Pak Study MCQs · pages 51-100

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

First Pakistani-born woman scientist to work in Max Planck Society is?

Correct Answer: Dr. Asifa Akhtar
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Dr. Asifa Akhtar is the first Pakistani-born woman scientist
  • Become a Director at the Max Planck Society
  • First international female Vice President of the Max Planck Society's Biology and Medicine Section.
  • Born in Karachi

Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026

2

What is the Original name of Mirza Ghalib?

Correct Answer: Mirza Asadullah Baig Khan
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Original Name: Mirza Asadullah Beg Khan
  • He  is one of the greatest Urdu and Persian poets.
  • Birth Date: December 27, 1797.
  • Birthplace: Kala Mahal, Agra, India.
  • He passed away on 15 February 1869 in Delhi.
  • Resting place: Mazar-e-Mirza Ghalib TombDelhi
  • Pen name: Ghalib, Asad 
  • Notable work: Diwan-e-Ghalib
  • Mirza Abdullah Baig (father)
  • Izzat-ut-Nisa Begum (mother)

Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026

3

Bauxite is extracted from which city of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Khushab
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Bauxite is an ore of aluminum
  • Bauxite deposits in Pakistan are primarily found in the
    • Salt Range (Khushab district),
    • Kotli (Azad Kashmir),
    • Margalla Hills/Hazara region.

Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026

4

Humayun-nama was written by?

Correct Answer: Gulbadan Begum
Show explanation
Explanation
  •  The Humayun Nama (History of Humayun) was written by Gulbadan Begum 
  • Original Composition: Completed around 1587.
  • Language: Simple Persian
  • English Translation Published Date: The most famous English translation by Annette S. Beveridge was published in 1902

Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026

5

How many barrages are stated to have been constructed on the Indus River from Kalabagh down to its delta?

Correct Answer: 6
Show explanation
Explanation

From Kalabagh to the Indus Delta, six major barrages are constructed on the Indus River.

  1. Chashma Barrage
  2. Taunsa Barrage
  3. Guddu Barrage
  4. Sukkur Barrage
  5. Kotri Barrage
  6. Jinnah Barrage

Additional information:

  • The Indus River is the national river of Pakistan.
  • It originates near Lake Mansarovar (Tibet region).
  • Flows through India and Pakistan.
  • Ends in the Arabian Sea near Thatta (delta region).
  • Ancient name: Sindhu
  • Total length: about 3,180 km
  • Indus River system is the backbone of Pakistan’s agriculture.
  • The upper Indus Plain starts near Kalabagh and ends at Mithankot.

Last verified on 07-05-2026

6

What percentage of the population of the State of Janmmu and Kashmir is stated as Muslim in the chapter?

Correct Answer: 77%
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Jammu and Kashmir had a Muslim majority population.
  • According to the chapter, about 77% population is Muslim.
  • This demographic factor was important in the region’s historical context.

Last verified on 07-05-2026

7

What is the meaning of the word Karakoram?

Correct Answer: Black rock
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The word Karakoram is a Turkic term.
  • It means “black gravel” or “black stone”.
  • It refers to the rocky and dark mountains of the region.
  • Karakoram is a mountain range located between Pakistan and China.
  • Karakoram Highway connects Pakistan and China.
  • Also called the Friendship Highway.
  • Passes through Khunjerab Pass.
  • Length in Pakistan: about 1,300 km.
  • Part of the historic Silk Route.

Last verified on 07-05-2026

8

What was Pakistan's rural population percentage according to the 2017 census table?

Correct Answer: 63.6%
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Rural population → 63.6%
  • Urban population → 36.4%
  • Total population → ~207 million
  • Male population → Slightly higher than female
  • Growth rate → Around 2.4%

Last verified on 07-05-2026

9

Who moved the resolution after the speech of Quaid-e-Azam according to the text?

Correct Answer: Sher-e-Bengal Molvi Fazl-ul-Haq
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The resolution refers to the Lahore Resolution (1940).
  • After the speech of Muhammad Ali Jinnah, the resolution was moved.
  • It demanded separate Muslim states in British India.
  • The Lahore Resolution is also called the Pakistan Resolution.
  • It was officially moved by A.K. Fazlul Huq (Sher-e-Bengal).

Last verified on 07-05-2026

10

Which factor intensified political unrest in East Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Language controversy
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Language issue was a major cause of unrest in East Pakistan.
  • The demand to impose Urdu created strong resistance from Bengali speakers.
  • This increased political tension and dissatisfaction.
  • Pakistan had two wings (East and West Pakistan) until 1971.

Last verified on 07-05-2026

11

Which governance issue widened the gap between East and West Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Centralized decision-making
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Power was concentrated in West Pakistan.
  • East Pakistan had less political and administrative control.
  • This unequal system increased mistrust and division.
  • Pakistan was divided into East and West Pakistan from 1947 to 1971.
  • These governance issues contributed to the separation in 1971, leading to the creation of Bangladesh.

Last verified on 07-05-2026

12

Gwadar Port is strategically important because it?

Correct Answer: Connects regional trade
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Gwadar Port is located on the Arabian Sea.
  • It connects Pakistan with Central Asia, Middle East, and China.
  • It plays a key role in international and regional trade.

Last verified on 07-05-2026

13

Dams in Pakistan mainly serve which combined purpose?

Correct Answer: Irrigation and power
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Dams in Pakistan store river water.
  • They are mainly used for irrigation (farms) and hydroelectric power generation.
  • They help agriculture and electricity supply at the same time.

Last verified on 07-05-2026

14

Pakistan's proximity to Central Asia enhances?

Correct Answer: Trade potential
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Pakistan is geographically close to Central Asian countries.
  • This location increases trade and economic opportunities.
  • It helps in connecting South Asia with Central Asia.

Central Asia is rich in:

  • Energy resources (oil, gas)
  • Trade routes

Last verified on 07-05-2026

15

Which natural region faces water scarcity despite large area?

Correct Answer: Balochistan Plateau
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Balochistan Plateau is the largest natural region of Pakistan by area.
  • Important water storage systems there include dams and karez irrigation systems
  • It receives very little rainfall and has limited water resources.
  • Therefore, it faces serious water scarcity problems.

Last verified on 07-05-2026

16

Monsoon winds primarily influence which aspect of Pakistan's climate?

Correct Answer: Rainfall distribution
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Monsoon winds bring seasonal rains to Pakistan.
  • They mainly affect the amount and distribution of rainfall.
  • Most monsoon rains occur during summer months.
  • Southwest monsoon winds come from the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal.
  • Punjab and northern areas receive more monsoon rainfall.

Last verified on 07-05-2026

17

Which factor contributes most to cultural diversity in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Regional variations
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Pakistan has different regions with unique cultures and traditions.
  • Each province has its own language, dress, food, and customs.
  • These regional differences create rich cultural diversity.

Examples of Cultural Diversity:

  • Punjabi culture → Bhangra and Punjabi language
  • Sindhi culture → Ajrak and Sindhi language
  • Balochi culture → Traditional Balochi dress
  • Pashtun culture → Attan dance
  • Kashmiri culture → Handicrafts and Kashmiri shawls

Last verified on 07-05-2026

18

Which reasoning best supports the Two-Nation Theory?

Correct Answer: Distinct religious
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Two-Nation Theory stated that Muslims and Hindus were two separate nations.
  • The main reason was their different religions, cultures, and social systems.
  • This theory later became the basis for the creation of Pakistan.
  • Muhammad Ali Jinnah strongly promoted the Two-Nation Theory.
  • The Lahore Resolution of 1940 officially demanded separate Muslim states.

Last verified on 07-05-2026

19

Which political development most strengthened Muslim demand for autonomy?

Correct Answer: Congress ministries (1937)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • In 1937, the Congress Party formed ministries in several provinces of British India.
  • Many Muslims felt politically and culturally neglected under Congress rule.
  • This increased Muslim demand for separate identity and autonomy.
  • Muslims observed “Day of Deliverance” in 1939 after Congress ministries resigned.

Last verified on 07-05-2026

20

Which system is used to distribute river water for agriculture?

Correct Answer: Canal system
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Canal system is used to carry river water to farms.
  • It helps irrigate agricultural land in dry areas.
  • Pakistan has one of the world’s largest canal irrigation systems.
  • Pakistan’s agriculture mainly depends on the Indus Basin Canal System.
  • Important crops like wheat, rice, and cotton rely on canal irrigation.

Last verified on 07-05-2026

21

Which dam is mentioned in the chapter among Pakistan's largest dams?

Correct Answer: Mangla
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Mangla Dam is one of the largest dams in Pakistan.
  • It was constructed on the Jhelum River in the 1960s.
  • The dam is used for water storage, irrigation, and hydroelectric power.
  • It plays a vital role in Pakistan’s water and energy needs.

Additional information:

  • Tarbela Dam (Indus River)
  • Warsak Dam (Kabul River)
  • Hub Dam (Balochistan/Sindh)
  • Mirani Dam (Balochistan)

Last verified on 06-05-2026

22

Pakistan's sea ports are described in the text as useful for what purpose?

Correct Answer: Transit routes
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Pakistan’s sea ports, such as Karachi Port and Port Qasim, are mainly used for trade and transit routes.
  • They facilitate import and export of goods to different countries.
  • These ports connect Pakistan with global shipping networks.
  • Thus, they play a key role in the country’s economy and transportation system.

Last verified on 06-05-2026

23

What is the second-highest mountain in the world?

Correct Answer: K2
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Mount K2 is the second-highest mountain on Earth.
  • It is part of the Karakorum Range.
  • It is located on the China–Pakistan border between Baltistan in the Gilgit-Baltistan region of northern Pakistan.
  • K2, at 8,611 meters above sea level.
  • K-2 is Also Known As Godwin Austin.
  • 'K' standing for Karakoram.
  • K2 mountain was first measured by Godwin Austen in 1856.
  • The Silk Route is also known as the Karakoram Highway.

Additional information:

  • The first highest mountain on Earth is Mount Everest.
  • It is located in Nepal.
  • The new height of Mount Everest is 8,848.86m (29,032 feet).
  • Mount Everest was named after George Everest in 1856.

Last verified on 01-04-2026

24

Province Punjab cover how much total area of land in percentage?

Correct Answer: 26%
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Area of Punjab is 205,345 square kilometers.
  • It comprises 26% of Pakistan's total area.
  • Punjab's population is 56% of Pakistan's total population.
  • Punjab is the second-largest province area-wise.
  • Punjab has 42 districts and 10 divisions.
  • It is located in the northeastern part of Pakistan.
  • Last verified on 06-05-2026
    • 25

    At which place in Lahore was the annual session of the Muslim League held in March 1940?

    Correct Answer: Minto Park
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Date: 23 March 1940
    • Place: Minto Park, Lahore
    • Presided by Muhammad Ali Jinnah
    • Resolution presented by A. K. Fazlul Huq
    • Led to demand for separate Muslim homeland

    Last verified on 06-05-2026

    26

    Which interpretation best explains why internal discord became especially dangerous for Pakistan in 1971?

    Correct Answer: It created conditions that external forces could exploit
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In 1971, Pakistan faced severe internal political and social conflict, especially in East Pakistan.
    • This internal instability weakened national unity and governance.
    • As a result, external forces—particularly India—were able to take advantage of the situation.
    • Thus, internal discord became a major strategic vulnerability.

    Last verified on 06-05-2026

    27

    Real name of Sultan Salahuddin Ayyubi was?

    Correct Answer: Yousuf
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The real name of Sultan Salahuddin Ayyubi was Yusuf ibn Ayyub
    • He was a 12th-century Kurdish Muslim leader
    • Founded the Ayyubid dynasty
    • Served as the first Sultan of Egypt and Syria.
    • Salah ad-Din is an Arabic title (laqab) meaning "Righteousness of the Faith".
    • Birthplace: Born in Tikrit, modern-day Iraq, around 1137 CE.
    • Legacy: Famously recaptured Jerusalem in 1187 CE after the Battle of Hattin.

    Last updated by NS on 06 May 2026

    28

    The book presents the fall of Dhaka in December 1971 as the outcome of which combination?

    Correct Answer: Internal strife and direct military intervention by India
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The fall of Dhaka in December 1971 led to the creation of Bangladesh.
    • It resulted from internal political and civil conflict in East Pakistan.
    • Along with this, the military intervention by India played a decisive role.
    • This event ended the unity of Pakistan as it existed since 1947.
    • The surrender was signed at Raman Racecourse Dhaka.

    Last verified on 06-05-2026

    29

    In which year was the National Action Plan revised?

    Correct Answer: 2021
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Pakistan's security framework evolved from the 20-point 
      • National Action Plan, 2014 ·(post-APS attack) 
      • To Revised National Action Plan – 2021 ·focusing on non-kinetic measures, terror financing
      • National PVE Policy 2024: The root causes of extremism through community engagement and education.

    Last updated by NS on 06 May 2026

    30

    Port Bin Qasim was established chiefly to meet the requirements of which major industrial concern?

    Correct Answer: Pakistan Steel Mills
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Port Qasim was established in 1973 near Karachi.
    • Its main purpose was to handle raw material imports for heavy industry.
    • The biggest beneficiary was the Pakistan Steel Mills.
    • It helped in importing iron ore, coal, and other industrial goods.

    Last verified on 06-05-2026

    31

    Which is largest (longest) and important river of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Indus
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Indus River is the longest and most important river in Pakistan
      • Flow from Country China, India, Pakistan
      • Flows from State Ladakh, Punjab, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Sindh, Gilgit-Baltistan, Tibet
      • Sources of water Tibetan Plateau, Senge Zangbu, Gar River
      • Tributaries Sutlej River, Chenab River, Jhelum River, Ravi River,
      • River Sindh Length 3,180 km
      • Mouth of the Arabian Sea near Thatta

    Additional information 

    How many rivers flow in Punjab Province? 5

    How many rivers flow in Sindh Province? 4

    How many rivers flow in Balochistan Province? 7

    How many rivers flow in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province? 8

    How many rivers are present in Pakistan? 24


    Last updated by NS on 06 May 2026


    32

    In areas where the canal system is unavailable, which alternative combination is identified in the book as a source of irrigation?

    Correct Answer: Hill torrents, springs and wells/tubewells
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In non-canal areas of Pakistan, agriculture depends on alternative water sources.
    • These include hill torrents, natural springs, and wells/tubewells.
    • Such methods are common in arid and mountainous regions.
    • They help provide water where canal irrigation is not available.

    Last verified on 06-05-2026

    33

    During the Soviet occupation of Afghanistan, Pakistan's strategic value to major powers increased mainly because Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Offered access toward the Arabian Sea and key regional routes
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • During the Soviet-Afghan War, Pakistan became a key regional player.
    • Its location provided access to the Arabian Sea and important transit routes.
    • Major powers used Pakistan for logistics, aid, and strategic operations related to Afghanistan.
    • Thus, its geopolitical importance increased significantly.

    Last verified on 06-05-2026

    34

    According to Times Higher Education (THE), World University Rankings2026, Who is the No 1 university in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam University (Islamabad)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to the Times Higher Education (THE) World University Rankings 2026, Quaid-i-Azam University (QAU) in Islamabad is ranked as the No. 1 university in Pakistan.

    QAU is placed within the 401–500 band globally


    List of Top Universities of Pakistan in 2026

    1.  Quaid-i-Azam University (QAU), Islamabad
    2. .National University of Sciences & Technology (NUST), Islamabad
    3. Lahore University of Management Sciences (LUMS),
    4. University of the Punjab (PU),
    5. COMSATS University Islamabad
    6. Pakistan Institute of Engineering & Applied Sciences (PIEAS)
    7. University of Agriculture, Faisalabad (UAF) .
    8. UET Lahore
    9. Aga Khan University (AKU), Karac
    10. Government College University (GCU), Lahore

    Last updated by NS on 06 May 2026

    35

    Why has the Indus Plain historically supported denser settlement than many mountainous and plateau regions?

    Correct Answer: Its level surface and river-based fertility favor agriculture and habitation
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Indus Plain has flat land and fertile soil due to the Indus River system.
    • This makes it ideal for agriculture, water supply, and settlements.
    • In contrast, mountainous areas are difficult for farming and transport.
    • Thus, people prefer living in plains.

    Last verified on 06-05-2026

    36

    Which comparison most accurately reflects the influence of location on Pakistan's climate?

    Correct Answer: Northern highlands remain colder because of altitude, whereas coastal areas stay relatively moderate due to the sea
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Pakistan’s climate varies due to differences in altitude and proximity to the sea.
    • Northern areas like Gilgit-Baltistan are cold because of high mountains.
    • Coastal areas like Karachi have a moderate climate due to the Arabian Sea.
    • Thus, location plays a key role in temperature differences.

    Last verified on 06-05-2026

    37

    Which interpretation best explains how Pakistani society can display both cultural diversity and social cohesion at the same time?

    Correct Answer: A common Islamic orientation coexists with provincial languages, customs and traditions
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Pakistan is a diverse country with many regional cultures, languages, and traditions.
    • At the same time, Islam provides a shared belief system that unites people.
    • This balance creates both cultural diversity and national unity.
    • Thus, unity exists alongside diversity in Pakistani society.

    Last verified on 06-05-2026

    38

    Which development most strengthened the Muslim League's conclusion that constitutional safeguards alone would not adequately protect Muslim political identity?

    Correct Answer: Congress ministries' conduct after the 1937 elections
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • After the 1937 Indian provincial elections, the Indian National Congress formed ministries in several provinces.
    • Their policies were seen as biased against Muslims (e.g., Bande Mataram, Wardha Scheme).
    • This created fear among Muslims about their political future.
    • Thus, the All-India Muslim League concluded that safeguards alone were not enough.

    Last verified on 06-05-2026

    39

    Which factor primarily affects river water availability in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Glacier melting
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In Pakistan, most rivers originate from the Himalayan and Karakoram glaciers.
    • The melting of glaciers provides a major source of river water.
    • Seasonal temperature changes directly affect water flow in rivers.
    • Climate change is affecting glacier size and water availability in Pakistan.

    Last verified on 06-05-2026

    40

    The winner of the 2026 Pakistan Super League (PSL) final was which team?

    Correct Answer: Peshawar Zalmi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    About Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2026

    Edition: 11th

    Participating teams: 08

    Total Matches: 44

    Starting date: 23 March 2026

    Final date: 3 May 2026

    Champion: Peshawar Zalmi

    Runners:up: Hyderabad Kingsmen

    ***

    41

    Which rivers were given to India under Indus Waters Treaty 1960?

    Correct Answer: Sutlej, Beas, Ravi
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Signed date: 19 September 1960
    • Brokered by: World Bank

    Pakistan got the Western Rivers:

    1. Indus
    2. Jhelum
    3. Chenab

      India got the Eastern Rivers:

      1. Ravi
      2. Beas
      3. Sutlej

        Signatories:

        • Field Marshal Ayub Khan (Pakistan)
        • Jawaharlal Nehru (India)

        Place of signing: Karachi

        Effective from: 1 April 1960 (implementation period)

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        According to the chapter on geo-political importance, which oil-rich states lie to the west of Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: Gulf States
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Pakistan is located at a strategically important geo-political position.
        • To its west lie important oil-rich Gulf States.

        Gulf States (Oil-rich Countries West of Pakistan):

        • Saudi Arabia
        • United Arab Emirates (UAE)
        • Kuwait
        • Qatar
        • Oman

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        43

        What is the average height of the Karakoram Range according to the text?

        Correct Answer: 6,100 metres
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The Karakoram Range is one of the highest mountain ranges in the world.
        • Its average height is around 6,100 metres (20,000 feet) above sea level.
        • This range is located mainly in Pakistan, India, and China.

        Additional information:

        • K2 → 8,611 metres (2nd highest in world)
        • Gasherbrum I → 8,080 metres
        • Broad Peak → 8,051 metres
        • Gasherbrum II → 8,035 metres
        • Masherbrum → 7,821 metres

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        44

        According to the 2017 census, what was the population density of Pakistan per square kilometre?

        Correct Answer: 261
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Pakistan (2017): 261 persons/km²
        • 1998 census: around 166 persons/km²
        • Highest density: Punjab province
        • Lowest density: Balochistan province
        • Urban areas: much higher than rural areas

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        45

        Which event accelerated the separation process?

        Correct Answer: Military conflict 1971
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The separation of East Pakistan was finalized in 1971.
        • The key event that accelerated this process was armed conflict between East and West Pakistan.
        • The war created a rapid political and military breakdown.
        • It led to the creation of an independent state of Bangladesh.

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        46

        Which factor contributed to separation of East Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: Political inequality
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • East Pakistan (now Bangladesh) separated in 1971.
        • The main reason was lack of political representation and inequality.
        • Economic and administrative discrimination increased tensions.

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        47

        Which port handles the majority of Pakistan's trade?

        Correct Answer: Karachi
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Ports are vital for a country’s import and export activities.
        • Pakistan’s largest and busiest port is the Karachi Port.
        • It handles the majority of sea trade, including imports and exports.
        • It is located on the Arabian Sea and supports the national economy.

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        48

        Which system is vital for irrigation in Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: Canal system
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Irrigation is essential for Pakistan’s agriculture-based economy.
        • The main source of irrigation is the extensive canal network.
        • These canals distribute water from rivers to agricultural fields.
        • It supports major crops like wheat, rice, and cotton.

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        50

        Which region is most suitable for agriculture due to fertile plains?

        Correct Answer: Indus Plains
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The most fertile land in Pakistan is found in the plains formed by rivers.
        • The Indus Plains are rich in alluvial soil, ideal for farming.
        • Continuous water supply from rivers supports agriculture.
        • Most crops like wheat, rice, and cotton are grown here.

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        51

        Which factor most influences Pakistan's climate variation?

        Correct Answer: Latitude and topography
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Pakistan’s climate varies due to its geographical position and land features.
        • Latitude determines how much solar energy different areas receive.
        • Topography (mountains, plains, deserts) affects temperature and rainfall.
        • Northern highlands are cold, while southern areas are warmer.

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        52

        Which feature most strongly reflects Pakistani society?

        Correct Answer: Cultural diversity with unity
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Pakistani society is made up of many cultures, languages, and traditions.
        • Despite diversity, people share common values and national identity.
        • This unity in diversity is a key feature of Pakistan.
        • Different provinces contribute to rich cultural heritage.

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        53

        The Two-Nation Theory primarily emphasizes which distinction?

        Correct Answer: Religious identity
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The Two-Nation Theory states that Muslims and Hindus are two separate nations.
        • It is mainly based on differences in religion, beliefs, and way of life.
        • It rejects the idea of a single united nation in the subcontinent.
        • This concept became the foundation of Pakistan’s creation in 1947.

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        54

        Which factor best explains the demand for a separate Muslim state?

        Correct Answer: Religious identity preservation
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The demand for a separate Muslim state was mainly based on religious identity.
        • Muslims of the subcontinent wanted to preserve their religion, culture, and traditions.
        • They feared domination by the Hindu majority in a united India.
        • This idea was clearly presented in the Lahore Resolution.

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        55

        Which source contributes most significantly to water availability in Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: Rivers
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Pakistan’s water supply mainly depends on its river system.
        • The Indus River System is the primary source of water for agriculture and daily use.
        • It includes major rivers like Indus, Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, and Sutlej.

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        56

        Natural gas is primarily used for which major purpose in Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: Power generation and domestic use
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Natural gas is an important fossil fuel used widely in Pakistan.
        • It is mainly used for electricity production and household purposes.
        • It also supports industries and transportation in some cases.
        • Pakistan has major gas fields in Sui (Balochistan).
        • Sui gas was discovered in 1952.

        Last verified on 04-05-2026

        57

        Who was the founder of Daily English Newspaper Dawn?

        Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Dawn newspaper was founded by Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah.
        • It was established on 26 October 1941 in Delhi (British India).
        • The main purpose was to support the Muslim League’s political voice.
        • It later became a major English newspaper of Pakistan after independence.

        Last verified on 03-05-2026

        58

        Best marthiya imagery poet?

        Correct Answer: Mir Anis
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Mir Anis is considered the best marsiya imagery poet
        • He was an Indian Urdu poet.
        • Born: 1800, Faizabad, Oudh State, India
        • Died, 10 December 1874 (aged 74) Lucknow, India
        • Pen name: Anees انیس
        • Period: Mughal era
        • Genre: Marsiya, Rubai
        • Subject: Battle of Karbala

        Additional information

        • Marsiya is a form of elegiac poetry describing sorrowful events, especially Karbala.

        Last updated question by NS on 2 MAY 2026

        59

        Marwan was the son of?

        Correct Answer: Al-Hakam
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Marwan I the fourth Umayyad caliph
        • Founder of the Marwanid line
        • Father: Al-Hakam ibn Abi al-As
        • Mother: Amina bint Alqama
        • Reign: 684–685 CE

        Additional information 

        •  Marwan II (Marwan ibn Muhammad), he was the son of Muhammad ibn Marwan 

        Last updated question by NS on 2 MAY 2026

        60

        حاجی بغلول کس کا کردار ہے؟

        Correct Answer: منشی سجاد حسین
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        حاجی بغلول اردو کے مشہور طنز و مزاح نگار اور ناول نگار ہے۔

         یہ کردار منشی سجاد حسین کے مشہور ناول "حاجی بغلول" میں مرکزی حیثیت رکھتا ہے

        جو انیسویں صدی کے آخر میں لکھا گیا۔


        Last updated question by NS on 2 MAY 2026

        61

        The growth rate of population in Pakistan is: ______ % per year.

        Correct Answer: 2.4
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Pakistan’s population growth rate is about 2.4% per year.
        • It shows how fast the population is increasing annually.
        • Growth rate depends on birth rate, death rate, and migration.
        • Higher growth rate means rapid population increase and pressure on resources.

        Last verified on 02-05-2026

        62

        What is a Sir Creek?

        Correct Answer: Tidal Estuary / Inlet channel of water
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Sir Creek is a tidal estuary (inlet channel of water).
        • It is located on the border of India and Pakistan.
        • Length is about 96 km (60 miles).
        • It is part of the Indus River Delta system.
        • It flows into the Arabian Sea.

        Last verified on 02-05-2026

        63

        Which is the highest Railway Station in Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: Kan Mehtarzai
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Highest railway station in Pakistan is Kan Mehtarzai Railway Station.
        • Located in Balochistan, Pakistan.
        • Height is about 2,224 meters above sea level.
        • Opened in 1921 and closed in 1986.
        • It was part of the Quetta–Zhob railway line.

        Additional information:

        • Lahore Railway Station is the largest in Pakistan (area-wise).
        • Rohri has the longest railway platform in Pakistan.

        Last verified on 02-05-2026

        64

        Muslim Bagh is famous for ______ ?

        Correct Answer: Chromite
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Muslim Bagh is a town in Killa Saifullah District, Balochistan.
        • It is famous for Chromite deposits.
        • Located at an altitude of about 1,787 meters (5,863 ft).
        • Formerly known as Hindu Bagh.
        • Chromite is used in stainless steel production.

        Last verified on 02-05-2026

        65

        Blind Dolphins are found in _______ river in Pakistan.

        Correct Answer: Indus
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Blind dolphins are found in the Indus River.
        • They are also called Indus River Dolphin or “Bhulan”.
        • Scientifically described in 1853 by Richard Owen.
        • Known for side-swimming behavior.
        • It is one of the rare freshwater dolphin species.

        Last verified on 02-05-2026

        66

        What is the length of coastline of Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: 1058 km
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Length: Old – 1,046 km, New – 1,058 km (both accepted in MCQs)
        • Divisions: Sindh Coast – 270 km, Makran Coast – 720 km
        • Major Location: Most of the coastline is in Balochistan
        • Geographical Areas: Gwadar, Lasbela, Karachi, Thatta Coast
        • Borders: Thatta Coast from Korangi Creek to Sir Creek; Rann of Kutch from Badin to Tharparkar

        Last verified on 15-01-2026

        67

        The contribution of Thermal Power in Pakistan in 2002-03 is?

        Correct Answer: 68%
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Pakistan generates electricity from thermal, hydro, and other sources.
        • In 2002–03, thermal power was the dominant source of electricity.
        • It contributed about 68% of total electricity generation.
        • Thermal power mainly comes from oil, gas, and coal plants.
        • Hydropower was the second major source at that time with 30%.

        Last verified on 02-05-2026

        68

        Pakistan extends from 60°50′ to?

        Correct Answer: 77°50' E
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Western extent: 60°50′ E
        • Eastern extent: 77°50′ E
        • Latitude range: ~24°N to 37°N
        • Time zone: PST (UTC +5)
        • Hemisphere: Northern & Eastern

        Last verified on 02-05-2026

        69

        Why Quaid-e-Azam said that Kashmir is a life line of Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: All five major rivers of Pakistan originate from Kashmir.
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Muhammad Ali Jinnah called Kashmir the “life line of Pakistan” due to its water importance.
        • All major rivers of Pakistan originate from Kashmir region and Himalayas.
        • These rivers are essential for agriculture, drinking water, and electricity (hydropower).
        • Without Kashmir water resources, Pakistan’s economy would be seriously affected.
        • Hence, Kashmir is strategically and economically very important.

        Last verified on 02-05-2026

        70

        The most significant development of the 1930s which strengthened Muslim belief in the Two-Nation Theory was?

        Correct Answer: Misrule by Congress Ministries
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The biggest development in the 1930s was the misrule of Congress ministries (1937–1939).
        • Provinces ruled by Congress: 7 provinces (British India)
        • It strengthened the belief in the Two-Nation Theory.
        • Muslims felt their rights, culture, and identity were ignored under Congress rule.
        • This increased political distance between Hindus and Muslims.
        • Muslim League response: Day of Deliverance (1939)

        Last verified on 02-05-2026

        71

        Separate electorate was granted to Muslims in the year?

        Correct Answer: 1909
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The demand for separate electorate was raised by the All-India Muslim League in 1906.
        • First legal acceptance came in the Indian Councils Act 1909.
        • This act is also known as the Morley-Minto Reforms.
        • It was implemented on 12 March 1909.

        Additional information:

        • Introduced separate electorate for Muslims.
        • Increased the size of Central and Provincial Legislative Councils.
        • Central Council had about 60 total members (approx. structure).
        • Around 27 members were elected under the reformed system.
        • Provided reserved representation for Muslims through separate voting.

        Last verified on 02-05-2026

        72

        Separate electorate demand by Muslim League was put forward in ____?

        Correct Answer: 1906
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The demand for separate electorate was raised by the All-India Muslim League in 1906.
        • First legal acceptance came in the Indian Councils Act 1909.
        • This act is also known as the Morley-Minto Reforms.
        • It was implemented on 12 March 1909.

        Additional information:

        • Introduced separate electorate for Muslims.
        • Increased the size of Central and Provincial Legislative Councils.
        • Central Council had about 60 total members (approx. structure).
        • Around 27 members were elected under the reformed system.
        • Provided reserved representation for Muslims through separate voting.

        Last verified on 02-05-2026

        73

        What is total area of the Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: 881,913 km²
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The total area of pakistan 881,913 km².
        • Pakistan is a country in South Asia.
        • Pakistan world's second-largest Muslim population.

          Additional information:

          • Pakistan is the 33rd-largest country by area.
          • The total cultivated area in Pakistan is 25%.
          • The total forest area of Pakistan is estimated at 6.20 million hectares.
          • The forest cover of Pakistan is 5.7%.
          • In old books area of Pakistan is 796096 sq km.

          Last verified on 02-05-2026

          74

          "حجن بی" کا کردار کس کا ہے؟

          Correct Answer: منشی پریم چند
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          حجن بی کا کردار منشی سجاد حسین کا تخلیق کردہ ہے۔

          منشی سجاد حسین اردو صحافت و ادب میں مزاح اور ظرافت نگاری کے حوالے سے مشہور ہیں، اور ان کے ناولوں میں مختلف معاشرتی کردار ملتے ہیں۔


          Last updated question 2 May 2026 by NS

          75

          علامہ اقبال کی صحت سے متعلق بچوں کی مشہور نظم کون سی ہے؟

          Correct Answer: بچوں کی دعا
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          علامہ اقبال کی بچوں کے لیے لکھی گئی مشہور ترین نظم "بچے کی دعا" ہے۔

          اس نظم کا پہلا مصرعہ "لب پہ آتی ہے دعا بن کے تمنا میری" ہے، جو پاکستان کے اسکولوں میں روزانہ صبح کے وقت پڑھی جاتی ہے۔

          یہ نظم بچوں میں حب الوطنی اور علم کے حصول کا جذبہ پیدا کرتی ہے۔


          ****

          Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026

          76

          Rao Maldeo Rathore was king of?

          Correct Answer: Marwar (Jodhpur)
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Rao Maldeo Rathore was the powerful king of Marwar,
          • Known for fighting the Battle of Sammel (1544) against Sher Shah Suri
          • He was the son of Rao Ganga
          • Reign: 1531/1532 to 1562.
          • Dynasty: Rathore clan.

          ****

          Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026

          77

          Which of the following are Islamic organizations?

          Correct Answer: OIC and Muslim League
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          OIC (Organization of Islamic Cooperation)
          • Founded in 1969
          • The second-largest intergovernmental organization after the United Nations
          • Has 57 member states
          • Called the “collective voice of the Muslim world”

          Muslim League (All-India Muslim League)

          • Worked for the rights and interests of Muslims in India
          • Founder: Khwaja Salimullah
          • Founded: 30 December 1906
          • President: Muhammad Ali Jinnah 
          • Played a key role in the Pakistan Movement
          • Led to the creation of Pakistan in 1947

          ****

          Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026

          78

          The Badaber Air Base was leased to the United States from?

          Correct Answer: 1958 to 1970
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The Badaber Air Base was leased to the United States from 1958 to 1970.
          • Formerly known as Peshawar Air Station
          • President Ayub Khan authorized the 10-year lease.
          • Construction began in 1958, known as "Little USA."
          • Operations in 1959
          • It was vacated in July 1970

          Additional information

          • Purpose: The facility served as a listening post for the US6937 Communication Group, monitoring Soviet activities.

          ****

          Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026

          79

          The Movement for the Restoration of Democracy (MRD), was a political alliance in Pakistan founded by the political parties opposing the military government of _______?

          Correct Answer: Zia-ul-Haq
          Show explanation
          Explanation


          Movement for the Restoration of Democracy (MRD)

          • Type: Political alliance in Pakistan
          • Founded: 1981, to oppose General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq’s military regime
          • Leader: Benazir Bhutto of Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP)
          • Purpose: Restore democracy and challenge military dictatorship

          • Date of Death: November 2, 2017, from pneumonia

          81

          Quaid-e-Azam joined All India Muslim League in _____?

          Correct Answer: 1913
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah joined
          • The All India Muslim League joined in: 1913 until 1947
          • Age: He was 37 years old.
          • Indian National Congress in 1906 and left in 1920.

          Additional information

          The All India Muslim League was founded in 1906, with its first session held in Karachi in 1907.

          In 1927, the League was divided over the issue of joint electorates.

          Who became the fastest Pakistani batter to score 1200 international runs?

          Correct Answer: Babar Azam
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Babar Azam has become the second-fastest Asian in April 2023.
          • Fastest to 12,000 T20 Runs: Babar Azam (338 innings).
          • Peshawar Zalmi vs Karachi Kings, PSL 2026.
          • Surpassed Chris Gayle (343 innings) and Virat Kohli (360 innings) to break the record

          83

          The book "Hunter Par Hunter" was written by?

          Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • "Hunter Par Hunter" was written by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan.
          • Purpose: To rebut W.W. Hunter's The Indian Musalmans
          • Language: Urdu

          Additional information 

          • Original Publication: Sir Syed reviewed Hunter's book in The Pioneer and it was reprinted in the Aligarh Institute Gazette between 24 November 1871 and 23 February 1872.

          84

          Kashmir sold to Gulab Singh in ____?

          Correct Answer: 1846
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Under the Treaty of Amritsar in March 1846, the British government sold Kashmir to Gulab Singh for 7.5 million Nanakshahee rupees.
          • Gulab Singh was subsequently bestowed with the title of Maharaja.

          Last verified on 01-05-2026

          85

          "Give me blood, I will give you freedom". These words are attributed to _____?

          Correct Answer: Subbash Bose
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Name: Subhas Chandra Bose (Netaji)
            • Born: 23 January 1897 (Cuttack, India)
            • Organization: Indian National Army (INA)

            Famous slogans:

            1. “Give me blood, and I shall give you freedom”
            2. “Jai Hind”

            Death: 1945 (plane crash in Taiwan – disputed)

            86

            How many canals take off from the Kotri Barrage?

            Correct Answer: 4
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            Kotri Barrage (Ghulam Muhammad Barrage)
            Total canals: 4
            • Left bank canals: 3
            • Right bank canals: 1
            Location: Jamshoro–Hyderabad (Sindh)
            • River: Indus River
            • Completed: 1955

            Last verified on 01-05-2026

            87

            The Legal Framework Order 1970 was issued by ______?

            Correct Answer: Yahya Khan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Legal Framework Order (LFO) was issued by two different individuals in Pakistan's history:

            • 1. General Yahya Khan in 1970.
            • 2. General Pervez Musharraf in 2002.

            The first LFO was used to establish the legal framework for the 1970 general elections.

            The second LFO, issued by Musharraf, made constitutional amendments and extended his presidential term.

            Last verified on 07-02-2026

            88

            Under the Constitution of 1962, how many seats were reserved for women in the National Assembly?

            Correct Answer: 6
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Constitution of Pakistan 1962

            • Women seats: 6
            • Total Assembly: 156 members (150 + 6)
            • Division: 3 East + 3 West Pakistan
            • Election: Indirect

            Additional information:

            Constitution of Pakistan 1956

            • Women seats: 10
            • Total Assembly: 310 members
            • Division: 5 East Pakistan + 5 West Pakistan
            • System: Parliamentary

            Constitution of Pakistan 1973

            • Originally women seats: 10
            • Later increased: 20 (1985) → 60 (since 2002)
            • Current system: Proportional representation
            • Total Assembly (today): 336 seats

            Last verified on 01-05-2026

            89

            Third Battle of Panipat was fought in?

            Correct Answer: 1761
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The Third Battle of Panipat was fought on 14 January 1761.
            • It took place at Panipat (near Delhi, India).
            • The battle was between the Maratha Empire and Ahmad Shah Durrani’s Afghan forces.
            • The Marathas were led by Sadashivrao Bhau.
            • The result was a decisive victory for Ahmad Shah Durrani.

            Additional information:

            • 1st Battle: 1526 (Babur vs Ibrahim Lodhi)
            • 2nd Battle: 1556 (Akbar vs Hemu)

            Last verified on 01-05-2026

            What is the length of the coastal line of Sindh, Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: 250 km / 200 miles
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Sindh coastline: 250 km / 200 miles
            • Total Pakistan coastline: 1,046 km
            • Longest coastline: Balochistan (~800 km)
            • Sea: Arabian Sea
            • Main coastal city: Karachi

            91

            What was the age of Liaquat Ali Khan at the time of his assassination?

            Correct Answer: 56 years
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Liaquat Ali Khan was Pakistan’s first Prime Minister.
            • Birth: 1 October 1895
            • Death: 16 October 1951
            • Age: 56 years
            • Place of assassination: Company Bagh (Liaquat Bagh), Rawalpindi
            • Title: Shaheed-e-Millat (Martyr of the Nation)
            • His assassin Syed Akbar was immediately killed on the spot by police.

            92

            Which is the first dry port of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Lahore
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • First dry port: Lahore Dry Port
              • Year: 1973
              • Authority: Pakistan Railways
              • Location: Mughalpura, Lahore
              • Purpose: Inland cargo/custom clearance

              Additional information:

              • Karachi Dry Port (1974)
              • Quetta Dry Port (1984)
              • Peshawar Dry Port (1986)
              • Multan Dry Port (1988)
              • Rawalpindi Dry Port (1990)

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              93

              Name the Assembly which passed the Act of One Unit, merging all the provinces of West Pakistan into one unit?

              Correct Answer: 2nd Constituent Assembly
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Assembly: 2nd Constituent Assembly
              • Bill passed: 30 September 1955
              • Implementation date: 14 October 1955
              • Provinces merged: 4 (Punjab, Sindh, NWFP, Balochistan)
              • Result: Formation of One Unit (West Pakistan)

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              94

              According to the partition plan the decision of accession of which province was left to the Provincial Assemblies?

              Correct Answer: All of these
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Plan: 3rd June / Mountbatten Plan
              • Year: 1947
              • Provinces involved: Punjab, Bengal, Sindh
              • Decision body: Provincial Assemblies
              • Choice: India or Pakistan

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              95

              The 3rd June plan also called as _____?

              Correct Answer: Mountbatten Plan
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The 3rd June Plan 1947 is also called the Mountbatten Plan.
              • It announced the partition of British India into India and Pakistan.
              • Punjab and Bengal were to be divided under this plan.
              • It was the final plan for independence.
              • It led directly to the creation of Pakistan in August 1947.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              96

              Lord Mountbatten replaced ______ as Viceroy of India in 1947.

              Correct Answer: Lord Wavell
              Show explanation
              Explanation
            • Lord Mountbatten replaced Lord Wavell as Viceroy of India in 1947.
            • He oversaw the partition of India and Pakistan's independence on August 14-15, 1947.
            • Last verified on 01-05-2026
              • 97

              The "Communal Award" given by?

              Correct Answer: Ramsay Macdonald
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The Communal Award was announced in August 16, 1932.
              • It was given by Ramsay MacDonald.
              • It introduced separate electorates for different communities.
              • Aimed to provide political representation to minorities.
              • It came after the 3rd Round Table Conferences.

              Last verified on 29-04-2026

              98

              When did the Muslim League decide to accept the Cabinet Mission plan?

              Correct Answer: June 6, 1946
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Acceptance date: 6 June 1946
              • Plan: Cabinet Mission Plan
              • Party: Muslim League
              • Opposing party issues: Congress disagreements
              • Outcome: Plan failed to succeed fully

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              99

              The Government of India Act of 1919 was mainly based on?

              Correct Answer: Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The Government of India Act 1919 was based on the Montagu–Chelmsford Reforms.
              • Introduced by Edwin Montagu and Lord Chelmsford.
              • It introduced the system of Dyarchy (dual government) in provinces.
              • Some powers were given to Indians in governance.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              100

              Who wrote Asar-ul-Sanadid in 1847?

              Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan
              Show explanation
              Explanation
            • Asar-ul-Sanadeed: A historical and architectural account of Delhi by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan.
            • First edition published in 1847.
            • Second edition published in 1854.
            • Last verified on 01-05-2026
              • 101

              Name the city which was the central point of agitation against the partition of Bengal?

              Correct Answer: Calcutta
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The agitation against the Partition of Bengal 1905 was centered in Calcutta.
              • Calcutta was the political and intellectual hub of Bengal.
              • The movement included boycotts and protests (Swadeshi Movement).
              • It strongly opposed the decision by Lord Curzon.
              • The agitation led to the reversal of partition in 1911.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              102

              The First Urdu Newspaper was?

              Correct Answer: Jamm e Jahan Numa
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The first Urdu newspaper called "Jam-e-Jahan-Numa"
              • It was published from Calcutta (now Kolkata) in 1822.
              • It marked the beginning of Urdu journalism in the subcontinent.
              • The Editor of Jam-i-Jahan-Numa by Harihar Dutta.

              Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026

              103

              Just after partition, when did India cut off Pakistan's water supply (stop headworks of Pakistani canals) without notice thus creating drought like conditions?

              Correct Answer: 1st April 1948
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • India began halting water supplies to Pakistani canals on April 1, 1948, after a short-term standstill agreement expired.
              • The halt started at the Ferozepur Headworks, affecting the Dipalpur Canal and other Pakistani canals.
              • This event led to water disputes between India and Pakistan, culminating in the Indus Waters Treaty in 1960.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              104

              An ordinance which had privileged by General Ayub Khan in 1959 was?

              Correct Answer: EBDO
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Elective Bodies Disqualification Order (EBDO) was introduced by Ayub Khan in August 1959.
              • It aimed to disqualify politicians accused of corruption or misconduct.
              • About 75 political leaders were affected.
              • Disqualification period was 8 years (till 1966).
              • It restricted many leaders from taking part in politics.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              105

              Kallar Kahar lake is located in _____?

              Correct Answer: Salt range
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Kallar Kahar  is a town and subdivision of Chakwal District in Punjab, Pakistan.
              • It is the capital of Kallar Kahar Tehsil.
              • Range: Salt Range
              • Features: Salt water lake, natural gardens, dancing peacocks, spiritual sites
              • Altitude: 1,500 ft above sea level
              • Area: 8 km
              • Maximum depth: 4–5 ft

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              106

              Lalusar Lake is situated in ____?

              Correct Answer: Kaghan Valley
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Lulusar Lake is located in Kaghan Valley (KPK).
              • It lies about 48 km from Naran.
              • The lake is at an ارتفاع of 3,410 meters (11,190 ft).
              • It is the main source of the Kunhar River.
              • Known for its natural beauty and tourism.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              107

              Who was the first editor of the Pakistan Times?

              Correct Answer: Faiz Ahmad Faiz
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Faiz Ahmed Faiz was the first editor of the newspaper Pakistan Times.
              • The newspaper started on 4 February 1947.
              • It was one of the leading English newspapers of early Pakistan.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              108

              Pakistan joined ASEAN in?

              Correct Answer: 2004
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) was founded on 8 August 1967.
              • Pakistan is not a member state of ASEAN.
              • Pakistan became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner in 1993.
              • It signed the Treaty of Amity and Cooperation (TAC) in 2004.
              • Pakistan joined the ASEAN Regional Forum (ARF) in 2004.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              109

              Ayub Khan's land reforms were an attempt to enhance the status of?

              Correct Answer: Both B and C
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The Ayub Khan introduced land reforms in 1959.
              • Main aim was to reduce large land ownership of landlords.
              • Land was redistributed to tenants (bataai-daar kisan) and landless farmers.
              • A limit was set on land ownership (500 acres irrigated, 1000 acres non-irrigated).
              • Objective was to improve agricultural productivity and rural equality.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              110

              Runn of Kutch is boundary line is known as?

              Correct Answer: 24th Parallel
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The Rann of Kutch boundary dispute is between Pakistan and India.
              • According to Pakistan, the boundary line is the 24th Parallel.
              • This area lies in Gujarat (India) and Sindh border region.
              • The dispute led to skirmishes before the 1965 war.
              • India does not officially recognize this line.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              111

              The 18th Amendment in the 1973 Constitution has abolished the?

              Correct Answer: Concurrent Legislative List
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The Eighteenth Amendment to the Constitution of Pakistan was passed in April 08, 2010.
              • It abolished the Concurrent Legislative List.
              • This increased provincial autonomy in Pakistan.
              • Many powers were transferred from federation to provinces.
              • It is considered a major step in strengthening federalism.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              112

              How many articles were there in the 1962 Constitution?

              Correct Answer: 250
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • 1962 Constitution: 250 articles, 12 parts, 3 schedules
              • It was introduced by Ayub Khan.
              • It introduced a presidential system of government.

              Additional information:

              • 1956 Constitution: 234 articles, 13 parts, 6 schedules
              • 1973 Constitution: 280 articles, 7 schedules + Objective Resolution

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              113

              The First Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was dissolved By Governor General Malik Ghulam Muhammad on ______?

              Correct Answer: October 24, 1954
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The first Constituent Assembly of Pakistan (1947–1954) was tasked with framing the constitution.
              • It was dissolved by Governor-General Ghulam Muhammad on 24 October 1954.

              Additional information:

              • Assembly formed: 10 August 1947
              • First Prime Minister: Liaquat Ali Khan
              • Constitution eventually passed: 1956

              Last verified on 07-02-2026

              114

              Pakistan constructed _____ canals under the Indus Basin Treaty.

              Correct Answer: 8
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Total link canals: 8
              • Treaty signed: 1960 (Pakistan & India, World Bank mediation)
              • Rivers for Pakistan: Indus, Jhelum, Chenab
              • Purpose: Water redistribution for irrigation
              • Major system: Indus Basin Irrigation Network

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              115

              Name two Indian provinces In which the Muslim League formed government during the war years of 1939-45?

              Correct Answer: Bengal and Sindh
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • During 1939–45, All India Muslim League formed governments in Bengal and Sindh.
              • These ministries were formed after the 1937 provincial elections.
              • The Congress won most provinces but failed in some key Muslim-majority areas.

              Total provinces: 11 British Indian provinces

              1. Madras
              2. Central Provinces
              3. Bihar
              4. Orissa
              5. United Provinces
              6. Bombay Presidency
              7. Assam
              8. NWFP
              9. Bengal
              10. Punjab
              11. Sindh

              Congress formed government in 8 provinces.

              Muslim League influence strong in 3 provinces:

              1. Bengal
              2. Sindh
              3. Punjab (no full ministry)

              Assam: Congress had 33/108 seats

              • The final results of the elections were declared in February 1937.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              116

              Who inaugurated Aligarh College?

              Correct Answer: Lord Lytton
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Anglo-Muhammadan Oriental College

              • Headquarters: Aligarh, India
              • Founded: 1875
              • Founder (idea/promoter): Sir Syed Ahmad Khan
              • Inaugurated by: Lord Lytton
              • Medium of instruction: English
              • Purpose: Modern education for Muslims

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              117

              In, 1861, the first railway line in Pakistan was laid between _____?

              Correct Answer: Karachi-Kotri
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Sir Henry Bartle Frere initiated railway development in Sindh.
              • The main goal was to connect Karachi port with inland areas.
              • The railway line was planned between Karachi and Kotri in April 1858.
              • It was opened on 13 May 1861 for public use.
              • Distance: about 174 km (108 miles).

              Last verified on 01-05-2026
              118

              The Queen's Proclamation was read at a Darbar, where that Darbar was held?

              Correct Answer: Allahabad
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The Queen's Proclamation 1858 was read after the end of the 1857 War of Independence.
              • It was read at a grand Durbar in Allahabad (1858).
              • The proclamation was announced by Lord Canning on behalf of Queen Victoria.
              • It marked the transfer of power from the East India Company to the British Crown.
              • This event officially started the British Raj in India.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              119

              Who started Jihad/stood against the Akbar's new religion Din-e-Illahi?

              Correct Answer: Mujadad Alif Sani
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Mujadad Alif Sani initiated the Jihad against Din-e-Illahi.
              • Sheikh Ahmad Sirhindi, also known as Imam Rabbani.
              • It is a religious movement introduced by Emperor Akbar in 1582.
              • Origin: Fatehpur Sikri, Agra, Mughal Empire
              • Members: 19
              • Other name Tawhid-i-Ilahi(توحید الهی)

              Last verified on 30-04-2026

              120

              The vale of Peshawar was the centre of the ancient Kingdom of?

              Correct Answer: Gandhara
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The Vale of Peshawar was the central region of the ancient Gandhara Civilization.
              • Gandhara flourished from 6th century BCE to 11th century CE.
              • Its main cities included Taxila and Pushkalavati (near Peshawar).
              • It was famous for Buddhist art and learning centers.
              • It connected South Asia with Central Asia through trade routes.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              121

              The author of "Introduction to Sindh its wealth and welfare" is?

              Correct Answer: M.B Pithawalla
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The book Introduction to Sindh: Its Wealth and Welfare was written by M. B. Pithawalla.
              • He was a well-known geographer and researcher of Sindh.
              • The book discusses Sindh’s natural resources, economy, and development.
              • It provides detailed insight into the wealth and welfare of the region.

              Last verified on 01-05-2026

              122

              Who is the Current Chief Minister (CM) of Gilgit-Baltistan(GB)?

              Correct Answer: Yar Muhammad
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              • Name: Justice (Retired) Yar Muhammad Nasir.

              • Title: Caretaker Chief Minister of Gilgit-Baltistan.
                Term Started: November 26, 2025
                Previous CM: Haji Gulbar Khan 

                Note: He was appointed for the role by Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif as the GB Assembly completed its five-year tenure on 23 Nov 2025.



                Last updated by NA on 27 April 2026

                123

                The Doctrine of Lapse was introduced by _____?

                Correct Answer: Lord Dalhousie
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • Lord Dalhousie took over as Governor General in 1848.
                • 'Doctrine of Lapse' was also formulated by Lord Dalhousie.
                • The Doctrine of Lapse was first applied in the year 1848 to the State of Satra.
                • The Doctrine of Lapse was introduced by Lord Dalhousie (1848-1856) in the year 1848.

                Last verified on 01-05-2026

                124

                Who described India as a beautiful bride whose two eyes were Muslims and Hindus?

                Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The statement is attributed to Sir Syed Ahmad Khan.
                • He described India as a beautiful bride.
                • According to him, its two eyes were Hindus and Muslims.
                • This metaphor highlighted the idea of communal harmony.
                • He promoted education and unity between communities.
                • He was the founder of the Aligarh Movement.

                Last verified on 01-05-2026

                125

                The largest source of tax revenue for the Government of Pakistan is?

                Correct Answer: Sales tax
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • In Pakistan, the largest share of tax revenue comes from indirect taxes.
                • The main contributor is Sales Tax, collected on goods and services.
                • It is administered mainly by the Federal Board of Revenue (FBR).

                Last verified on 01-05-2026

                126

                FATF has kept Pakistan on the grey list until June despite, the country has complied with ______ out of the 27 actions suggested by the FATF.

                Correct Answer: 24
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) monitors financial crimes.
                • Pakistan was kept on the grey list in 2021 (until June).
                • At that time, Pakistan had complied with 24 out of 27 actions.
                • These actions were related to terror financing and money laundering controls.
                • Later, compliance increased to 26 out of 27 actions by June 2021.

                Last verified on 01-05-2026

                127

                Section _____ of the Indian independence Act, 1947 dealt with the powers of Governor-General.

                Correct Answer: Ninth
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The Indian Independence Act 1947 defined the transfer of power to India and Pakistan.
                • Section 9 dealt with the powers of the Governor-General.
                • It allowed him to implement and operationalize the Act.
                • He could divide powers, assets, and responsibilities between the two dominions.
                • It helped in the smooth establishment of India and Pakistan in 1947.

                Last verified on 01-05-2026

                128

                Who was the first and last woman ruler of India (Delhi)?

                Correct Answer: Razia Sultana
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • Razia Sultana was the first and last female ruler of Delhi Sultanate.
                  • She ruled India from 1236 to 1240.
                  • She was the daughter of Sultan Shams-ud-Din Iltutmish and Turkan Khatun.
                  • Known for her bravery, intelligence, and administrative skills.

                  Last verified on 02-02-2026

                  129

                  Pakistan has a _____ Court system.

                  Correct Answer: Two sets of courts
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Pakistan has a two-tier (two sets) court system.
                  • It consists of Superior Judiciary and Subordinate Judiciary.
                  • Supreme Court of Pakistan and High Courts are part of the superior judiciary.
                  • Lower courts include civil and criminal courts at district level.
                  • This system ensures justice from lower to highest level.

                  Last verified on 01-05-2026

                  130

                  In order to benefit from the port at Karachi, Bartle Frere initiated a railway line scheme aimed at linking Karachi with?

                  Correct Answer: Kotri
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Sir Henry Bartle Frere initiated railway development in Sindh.
                  • The main goal was to connect Karachi port with inland areas.
                  • The railway line was planned between Karachi and Kotri in April 1858.
                  • It was opened on 13 May 1861 for public use.
                  • Distance: about 174 km (108 miles).

                  Last verified on 01-05-2026

                  131

                  Gazetteer of West Pakistan: The Province of Sindh including Khairpur State is the work is?

                  Correct Answer: H.T Sorley
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • The book Gazetteer of West Pakistan: The Province of Sindh including Khairpur State was written by H. T. Sorley.
                  • It was published in 1959.
                  • It provides detailed information about Sindh’s history, geography, culture, and society.
                  • It also includes data about Khairpur State.

                  Correct Answer: Josef Korbel
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Josef Korbel was the father of Madeleine Albright.
                  • He served in the UN Commission on India and Pakistan (UNIP).
                  • He also wrote the book Danger in Kashmir.
