                  133

                  The Constitution of Pakistan 1956 introduced a which legislature/system?

                  Correct Answer: Unicameral
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • The Constitution of Pakistan 1956 introduced a unicameral legislature.
                    • It was called the Parliament of Pakistan.
                    • It consisted of only one house (National Assembly).

                    Additional information:

                    • 1962 Constitution: Presidential system with changes in structure
                    • 1973 Constitution: Bicameral Parliament (National Assembly + Senate)

                    Last verified on 01-05-2026

                    134

                    UNHCR in Pakistan works for?

                    Correct Answer: Rehabilitation of refugees
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Full Form: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees
                    • Founded: 1950
                    • Headquarters: Geneva, Switzerland
                    • Main Role: Refugee protection & rehabilitation
                    • Presence: Active in Pakistan for decades

                    Last verified on 01-05-2026

                    135

                    The court language of the Mughals in India was _______?

                    Correct Answer: Persian
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The official court language of the Mughal Empire was Persian.
                    • It was used in administration, literature, and royal communication.
                    • Mughal rulers adopted Persian from earlier Islamic traditions.
                    • It remained dominant until the British period.
                    • It influenced the development of Urdu language.

                    Last verified on 01-05-2026

                    136

                    The strongholds of Arya Samaj were mainly in the?

                    Correct Answer: Western and Northern India
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Arya Samaj was a Hindu reform movement.
                    • It was founded by Swami Dayanand Saraswati in 1875.
                    • Its main strongholds were in Western and Northern India.
                    • Key areas included Punjab, Uttar Pradesh, and parts of Rajasthan.
                    • It promoted Vedic teachings and social reforms.

                    Last verified on 01-05-2026

                    137

                    The body of law which defines the relationship of the different entities within a state namely the legislature, the executive and the judiciary is called?

                    Correct Answer: Constitutional Law
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Constitutional Law defines the structure of a state.
                    • It explains the relationship between legislature, executive, and judiciary.
                    • It is based on the constitution of a country.
                    • It also defines powers, duties, and limits of institutions.
                    • It protects fundamental rights of citizens.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    138

                    According to the Article _____ of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, Islam shall be the state religion of Pakistan.

                    Correct Answer: Article 2
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The Constitution of Pakistan clearly defines the state religion.
                    • Under Article 2, Islam is declared the state religion of Pakistan.
                    • This provision was included in the Constitution of 1973.
                    • It reflects the Islamic identity of the state.
                    • It guides the overall legal and ideological framework of Pakistan.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    139

                    Which of the following in Pakistan is supreme policy-making body in the economic sphere?

                    Correct Answer: NEC
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Full Form: National Economic Council (NEC)
                    • Chairman: Prime Minister
                    • Article: 156
                    • Function: Economic planning & policy-making
                    • Members: Federal & Provincial representatives

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    140

                    Which of the following is the power and function of Supreme Judicial Council? Settling disputes between Federal and Provincial Goverovment, Hearing the cases of misconduct of Judges, Hearing appeals from other courts, All of these

                    Correct Answer: Hearing the cases of misconduct of Judges
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The Supreme Judicial Council ensures accountability of judges in Pakistan.
                    • It deals with misconduct cases against judges of superior courts.
                    • Established under Article 209 of the Constitution of Pakistan.
                    • It can recommend removal of judges if found guilty.
                    • It maintains judicial discipline and integrity.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    141

                    _____ UN General Assembly President who welcomed India-Pakistan's recent agreement to observing the ceasefire agreement at LOC.

                    Correct Answer: Volkan Bozkir
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Volkan Bozkir welcomed the India-Pakistan ceasefire agreement.
                    • He was the President of the UN General Assembly (2020–2021).
                    • The ceasefire relates to the Line of Control (LoC) between India and Pakistan.
                    • The development was seen as a positive step for regional peace.
                    • The UN encourages dialogue and peaceful conflict resolution.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    142

                    As early as 1913, Muslim League formally adopted the following objective of the congress?

                    Correct Answer: Self-Government for India within the Empire
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • In 1913, the All India Muslim League adopted a moderate political objective.
                    • It supported self-government (autonomy) for Indians within the British Empire.
                    • This reflected early cooperation between Muslim League and Congress ideals.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    143

                    Who introduced firstly the silver coin (rupee) named "Rupia" in the Indian subcontinent?

                    Correct Answer: Sher Shah Suri
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • During his five-year rule from 1540 to 1546.
                    • Sher Shah Suri set up a new civic and military administration.
                    • He issued a coin of silver, weighing 178 grains.
                    • It was also termed the Rupiya.
                    • The silver coin remained in use during the Mughal period, the Maratha era (1674–1818) and in British India, as well.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    144

                    Who described in Musaddas, the rise of and fall of Islam as a political and cultural force in the world?

                    Correct Answer: Altaf Hussain Hali
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The famous poem Musaddas-e-Hali was written by Altaf Hussain Hali.
                    • Written in 1879.
                    • It describes the rise and fall of Muslims as a political and cultural power.
                    • The work highlights the glorious Islamic past and later decline.
                    • He was a close associate of Sir Syed Ahmad Khan.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    145

                    IMF gave one-year relief to Pakistan and US$ 1.386 billion were give under the _____ to address the economic effects of Covid-19.

                    Correct Answer: Rapid Financing Instrument
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The International Monetary Fund (IMF) provided emergency financial support to Pakistan during COVID-19.
                    • In 2020, Pakistan received US$ 1.386 billion assistance.
                    • This support was given under the Rapid Financing Instrument (RFI).
                    • It was aimed at addressing the economic impact of COVID-19.
                    • The facility is used for urgent balance of payment needs.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    146

                    Pakistan faces challenge of maintaining balance in its friendly relations with China mainly because of the following?

                    Correct Answer: US anti-China belligerence
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Pakistan has strong strategic relations with China due to economic and regional interests.
                    • However, global geopolitics creates balancing challenges for Pakistan.
                    • The main issue is increasing tension between China and United States.
                    • US anti-China policies and rivalry put Pakistan in a diplomatic balancing position.
                    • Pakistan tries to maintain good relations with both major powers.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    147

                    "Abolition of Jagirdari: An explanation is written by?

                    Correct Answer: Ali Muhammad Rashidi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The book Abolition of Jagirdari: An Explanation was written by Ali Muhammad Rashidi.
                    • It was published in 1955.
                    • He wrote it while serving as Sindh’s Revenue Minister.
                    • It explains the end of the feudal (Jagirdari) system in Sindh/Pakistan.
                    • Published by the Directorate of Information, Sindh.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    148

                    The issue of separation of Sindh from Bombay remained dormant until the arrival of?

                    Correct Answer: Simon Commission
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The demand for separation of Sindh from Bombay Presidency remained mostly inactive for some time.
                    • It became active again during the British constitutional reforms process.
                    • The issue gained importance with the arrival of the Simon Commission in 1927.
                    • Finally, Sindh was separated in 1936 as a separate province.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    149

                    NCOC stands for ______?

                    Correct Answer: National Command and Operation Centre
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The early response to COVID-19 in Pakistan (2020) was led by National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA).
                    • Later, a central body was created called National Command and Operation Center (NCOC).
                    • NCOC was established in April 2020 on the orders of Prime Minister Imran Khan.
                    • It coordinated national efforts against COVID-19.
                    • NCOC was officially dissolved on 31 March 2022.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    150

                    Who started the Faraizi Movement?

                    Correct Answer: Haji Shariat Ullah
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The Haji Shariatullah started the Faraizi Movement in 1818.
                    • It was a religious and social reform movement in Bengal.
                    • The aim was to encourage Muslims to strictly follow Islamic duties (Faraiz).
                    • He was called Ustad (teacher) by his followers.
                    • After Haji Shariatullah, his son Dudu Miyan continued the movement.

                    Last verified on 29-04-2026

                    151

                    Who said: "Had Muhammad Ali Jinnah died earlier, there would not have been Pakistan"?

                    Correct Answer: Michael Brecher
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • This statement is attributed to Michael Brecher.
                    • He mentioned it in his book Jawaharlal Nehru: A Political Biography.
                    • He discussed the central role of Muhammad Ali Jinnah in the creation of Pakistan.
                    • According to him, Jinnah’s leadership was crucial for Pakistan’s formation in 1947.
                    • The idea highlights how individual leadership shaped history.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    152

                    Under the Article _____ of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, Supreme Court shall, to the exclusion of every other Court, have original jurisdiction in any dispute between any two or more Governments.

                    Correct Answer: Article 184
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Under the Constitution of Pakistan, the Supreme Court has special original jurisdiction.
                    • It deals with disputes between federal government and provincial governments.
                    • It also covers disputes between provinces themselves.
                    • This power is given under Article 184 of the Constitution.
                    • It ensures the Supreme Court acts as the highest authority in inter-government disputes.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    153

                    The Shah Bano controversy was?

                    Correct Answer: A landmark legal battle that raised debate over Muslim personal law and secularism
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The Shah Bano case was a famous legal case in India (1985).
                    • It was related to a Muslim woman’s right to maintenance after divorce.
                    • The Supreme Court gave a judgment in her favor under criminal law.
                    • It created a major debate on Muslim Personal Law vs secular law.
                    • It became a landmark case in women’s rights and constitutional law.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    154

                    "Partition: The Story of Indian Independence and the Creation of Pakistan in 1947" is written by?

                    Correct Answer: B.W Spunner
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The book is written by Barney White-Spunner.
                    • It was published in 2017.
                    • It describes the Partition of India in 1947 and creation of Pakistan.
                    • Focuses on events from January to August 1947 in detail.
                    • It explains the political decisions and violence during Partition.

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    155

                    The function of NACTA is to?

                    Correct Answer: Eliminate terrorism
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Full Form: National Counter Terrorism Authority
                    • Headquarters: Islamabad, Pakistan
                    • Established: 2009 (initial setup: January 2008)
                    • Legal Formation: 2013
                    • Minister Responsible: Interior Minister
                    • Motto: Stand United Against Terrorism
                    • Purpose: Counter terrorism & extremism

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    156

                    Islamabad was declared the Capital of Pakistan in ______?

                    Correct Answer: 1960
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Islamabad was named on 24 February 1960.
                    • It was declared the new capital of Pakistan during the era of Ayub Khan.
                    • The capital was shifted from Karachi to Islamabad for better administration and a central location.
                    • The city was planned and designed by the Greek architect Constantinos Apostolou Doxiadis.

                    Additional information:

                    • 1961 – Construction of the new capital city started
                    • 1967 – Government offices gradually shifted to Islamabad

                    Last verified on 16-03-2026

                    157

                    In which year, the construction of Mangla Dam was completed?

                    Correct Answer: 1967
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Mangla Dam was completed on 23 November 1967.
                    • Its construction started in 1962.
                    • It is built on the Jhelum River in Mirpur District, Azad Kashmir.
                    • It is an earth-filled embankment dam.
                    • Height – 147 meters
                    • Length – 3,140 meters

                    Last verified on 30-04-2026

                    158

                    Pakistan National Movement was established by Ch. Rehmat Ali in the year?

                    Correct Answer: 1933
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Pakistan National Movement was founded by Choudhry Rahmat Ali.
                    • It was established in 1933.
                    • He was the first person to coin the name “Pakistan”.
                    • The idea was promoted through his famous pamphlet Now or Never.
                    • He aimed to demand a separate homeland for Muslims of India.

                    Last verified on 26-04-2026

                    159

                    When did Pakistan join SEATO?

                    Correct Answer: 1954
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • SEATO was founded on 8 September 1954.
                    • Pakistan joined SEATO in the same year 1954 as a member.
                    • It was a Cold War military alliance led by the United States.
                    • Pakistan later withdrew its association in the 1970s (after 1972/1973 period changes).
                    • SEATO was officially dissolved in 1977.

                    Last verified on 29-04-2026

                    160

                    Which Five year plan was not implemented due to political instability, and economic uncertainty?

                    Correct Answer: Fourth five-year plan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The Fourth Five-Year Plan (1970–1975 ) of Pakistan was not properly implemented.
                      • It was disrupted due to political instability in 1971.
                      • The fall of East Pakistan (Bangladesh creation) affected its execution.
                      • Government of Zulfikar Ali Bhutto revised economic policies.
                      • Therefore, the plan was largely abandoned or bypasses.

                      Additional information:

                      1. 1955–1960 → First Five-Year Plan
                      2. 1960–1965 → Second Five-Year Plan
                      3. 1965–1970 → Third Five-Year Plan

                      Last verified on 30-04-2026

                      161

                      The lower Indus plain lies between?

                      Correct Answer: Mithan Kot and Arabian Sea
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • Lower Indus Plain is a major agricultural region.
                        • It lies between Mithan Kot and the Arabian Sea.
                        • It is formed by the Indus River and its tributaries.
                        • It includes parts of Sindh and southern Punjab.
                        • Indus River length in Pakistan – about 2,900 km (approx.)

                        Additional information:

                        • Upper Indus Plain – Northern Punjab
                        • Middle Indus Plain – Central Punjab

                        Last verified on 30-04-2026

                        162

                        Lahore-Islamabad Motorway project was launched on?

                        Correct Answer: January 12, 1992
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation
                        • The M-2 Motorway (Lahore–Islamabad) project was launched on 12 January 1992.
                        • It was initiated during the government of Nawaz Sharif.
                        • 1997 – Motorway completed and opened.

                        Last verified on 30-04-2026

                        163

                        In which city of Pakistan Second Islamic Summit Conference was held?

                        Correct Answer: Lahore
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation
                        • Pakistan hosted the 2nd Islamic Summit Conference of the OIC from 22–24 February 1974 in Lahore.
                          • An Extraordinary OIC Summit was also held later in 1997 in Islamabad on Pakistan’s Golden Jubilee.

                        Additional information:

                        • 1st Summit: Rabat, Morocco – September 1969
                        • 3rd Summit: Makkah, Saudi Arabia – 25–28 January 1981

                        Last verified on 07-03-2026

                        164

                        The Indian Councils Act (Minto-Morley Reforms) was enacted into law in ____?

                        Correct Answer: 1909
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation
                        • The Indian Councils Act 1909, also called the Morley-Minto Reforms, introduced separate electorates for Muslims.

                        Additional information:

                        • Date of introduction: 12 March 1909
                        • Total members in Central Legislative Council: 60
                        • Muslim reserved seats: 5
                        • 27 members were to be elected from different constituencies.
                        • Nehru Report (28 August 1928) rejected the demand for separate electorate.

                        Last verified on 01-04-2026
                        165

                        Which system of transport is widely used by people of Pakistan?

                        Correct Answer: Road
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation
                        • In Pakistan, the most widely used transport system is road transport.
                        • It is used for daily travel, trade, and goods transportation.
                        • Roads connect cities, villages, and remote areas.

                        Last verified on 30-04-2026

                        166

                        The importance of Potwar Plateau is due to? Dandot Makarwal Coal mines, Khewra Salt mines, Kalabagh Salt mines, All of these

                        Correct Answer: All of these
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation
                        • Potwar Plateau is an important region of Pakistan.
                        • It is lies between Jhelum and Indus rivers.
                        • It is part of the Salt Range region of Punjab.

                          Important Resources of Potwar Plateau:

                          • Dandot Coal Mines
                          • Makarwal Coal Mines
                          • Khewra Salt Mine
                          • Kalabagh Salt Range
                          • Oil and gas reserves in some areas

                          Last verified on 30-04-2026

                          167

                          The lady represented AIML, supporting Pakistan Resolution was?

                          Correct Answer: Amjad Bano
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • Lahore Resolution was passed on 23 March 1940.
                          • Amjadi Bano Begum was the only woman who supported it in the session.
                          • She was the widow of Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar.
                          • She declared it as the “Pakistan Resolution” for the first time.
                          • Her speech was a historic contribution to the Pakistan Movement.

                          Last verified on 30-04-2026

                          168

                          Allama Iqbal gave his historical Presidential Address at ____?

                          Correct Answer: Allahabad
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • Allama Iqbal delivered his famous Presidential Address on 29 December 1930.
                          • It was held at the Allahabad session of the All-India Muslim League.
                          • November 9, 1877 – Born in Sialkot
                          • 1922 – Actively joined Muslim League politics
                          • April 21, 1938 – Died in Lahore

                          Last verified on 30-04-2026

                          169

                          Indian Councils Act of 1909 is also known as?

                          Correct Answer: Minto-Morley Reforms
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • The Indian Councils Act 1909, also called the Morley-Minto Reforms, introduced separate electorates for Muslims.

                          Additional information:

                          • Date of introduction: 12 March 1909
                          • Total members in Central Legislative Council: 60
                          • Muslim reserved seats: 5
                          • 27 members were to be elected from different constituencies.
                          • Nehru Report (28 August 1928) rejected the demand for separate electorate.

                          Last verified on 01-04-2026

                          170

                          Lord Curzon introduced reforms in agriculture and ______?

                          Correct Answer: Administration
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • Lord Curzon introduced reforms in agriculture and administration.
                          • He served as Viceroy of India (1899–1905).
                          • His reforms aimed to improve governance and efficiency.
                          • He also strengthened bureaucratic control and revenue system.
                          • His period is known for major administrative restructuring in British India.

                          Last verified on 30-04-2026

                          171

                          Urdu is a word of _____?

                          Correct Answer: Turkish
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • The word “Urdu” is derived from the Turkish word “Ordu”.
                          • In Turkish, Ordu means “Army” or “camp”.
                          • Urdu language developed in the Indian subcontinent.
                          • It became widely used as a lingua franca in South Asia.
                          • The name “Urdu” was first used around 1780 by Ghulam Hamadani Mushafi.
                          • Maulvi Abdul Haq – Known as “Father of Urdu”.

                          Last verified on 30-04-2026

                          172

                          Gwadar was built with the help of which country?

                          Correct Answer: China
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • Gwadar Port was developed with the help of China.
                            • It is located on the Arabian Sea in Balochistan.
                            • It is important for trade and regional connectivity.

                            Additional information:

                            • 1783 – Gwadar came under Oman
                            • 8 Sept 1958 – Pakistan purchased Gwadar from Oman
                            • 8 Dec 1958 – Gwadar became part of Pakistan
                            • 2002 – Gwadar Port opened
                            • 18 Feb 2013 – Port operations given to a Chinese company

                            Last verified on 30-04-2026

                            173

                            Criminal Law Amendment Bill was signed by?

                            Correct Answer: General Pervez Musharaf
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • The Criminal Law Amendment Bill was signed by Pervez Musharraf.
                            • It was part of legal reforms during his rule.
                            • Musharraf served as President of Pakistan (2001–2008).
                            • The amendment aimed to update and regulate criminal laws.
                            • It reflected efforts toward legal and administrative reforms.

                            Last verified on 30-04-2026

                            174

                            Chaudhary Rehmat Ali issued pamphlet as his solution to the Indian Communal Problems. It was called _____?

                            Correct Answer: Now or Never
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • First Introduced: 1933 by Chaudhary Rahmat Ali.
                            • Pamphlet: Titled "Now or Never" meaning Are We to Live or Perish Forever?

                            Additional information:

                            • First Use of Pakistan: Word appeared for the first time in this pamphlet.
                            • Based On: Two-nation theory of Syed Ahmed Khan.

                            Regions Represented (Pakistan):

                            1. Punjab
                            2. Afghanistan
                            3. Kashmir
                            4. Sindh
                            5. Baluchistan

                            Last verified on 24-03-2026

                            175

                            In 1931 Qaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah went in to self-exile in London. When he came back on the appeal of the Muslims?

                            Correct Answer: 1934
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • Muhammad Ali Jinnah went to London in 1931 (self-exile).
                            • He became disappointed with Indian politics at that time.
                            • Muslim leaders requested him to return and lead them.
                            • He came back to India in 1934.
                            • After return, he reorganized the All-India Muslim League.
                            • Jinnah is called “Quaid-e-Azam” (Great Leader).

                            Last verified on 30-04-2026

                            176

                            Who started the Silk Letter Movement (Reshmi Roomal Movement)?

                            Correct Answer: Both A and B
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • The Silk Letter Movement was started by Mahmud Hasan Deobandi and Ubaidullah Sindhi.
                            • It began around 1913 as a secret movement against British rule.
                            • Messages were written on silk cloth to avoid detection.
                            • The aim was to gain foreign support (Afghanistan, Turkey).
                            • It continued until 1920 but was later exposed by the British.

                            Last verified on 30-04-2026

                            177

                            The British Government took stern action against the "Quit India Movement" and put in to jail?

                            Correct Answer: Gandhi
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • The Quit India Movement was launched in August 1942.
                            • It was led by Mahatma Gandhi against British rule.
                            • The British government reacted with strict action and mass arrests.
                            • Gandhi and other leaders were immediately jailed.
                            • Muhammad Ali Jinnah did not support the Quit India Movement.
                            • The movement slogan was “Do or Die.”

                            Last verified on 30-04-2026

                            178

                            On which occasion Jinnah totally disillusioned Hindus and decided "Parting the ways"?

                            Correct Answer: All Parties Conference December 1928
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • Muhammad Ali Jinnah became disillusioned during the All Parties Conference (December 1928).
                            • The conference discussed the Nehru Report.
                            • Jinnah proposed amendments to protect Muslim rights, but they were rejected.
                            • This led him to feel that Hindus were not willing to accommodate Muslims.
                            • He then decided on “parting of the ways”, marking a turning point in history.
                            • Jinnah is called the “Ambassador of Hindu-Muslim Unity” before this event.

                            Last verified on 30-04-2026

                            179

                            Who divided Bengal into East and West Bengal (partitioned) in July 1905?

                            Correct Answer: Lord Curzon
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • The Partition of Bengal, implemented in 1905 by Lord Curzon.
                            • It was formally annulled in 1911. 
                            • He divided Bengal into two provinces on 16-10-1905.

                              The province was divided into:

                              • West Bengal, which had a majority of Hindus,
                              • East Bengal, which had a majority of Muslims

                              Last verified on 30-03-2026

                              180

                              Bengal was divided into two provinces in _____?

                              Correct Answer: 1905
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The Partition of Bengal was carried out on 16-10-1905 by Lord Curzon.
                              • Bengal was divided into East Bengal & Assam and West Bengal.
                              • The main purpose was administrative efficiency, but it had political effects.
                              • It created strong opposition from Hindus and support from many Muslims.
                              • The partition was later reversed in 1911.

                              Last verified on 30-04-2026

                              181

                              Indus civilization developed around _____?

                              Correct Answer: 3000 BC
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Indus Valley Civilisation (IVC)

                              • Also known as the Indus Civilisation.
                              • A Bronze Age civilisation in the northwestern regions of South Asia.
                              • Existed from 3300 BCE to 1300 BCE.
                              • Reached its mature phase between 2600 BCE and 1900 BCE.

                              Last verified on 30-04-2026

                              182

                              The people gathered in Jallianwala Bagh were protesting against?

                              Correct Answer: Rowlatt Act
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The gathering at Jallianwala Bagh in 1919 was a peaceful protest.
                              • People were protesting against the Rowlatt Act.
                              • The Act allowed arrest without trial and strict control over Indians.
                              • It also protested the arrest of leaders like Saifuddin Kitchlew and Satyapal.
                              • The protest led to the tragic Jallianwala Bagh massacre (13 April 1919).

                              Last verified on 29-04-2026

                              183

                              In ______ Pakistan and Iran signed an agreement for supply of 750 million cubic feet gas per day.

                              Correct Answer: 2009
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Pakistan and Iran signed the gas supply agreement in 2009.
                              • The deal was part of the Iran-Pakistan (IP) Gas Pipeline Project.
                              • Under this agreement, Iran would supply 750 million cubic feet gas per day.
                              • The project aimed to overcome Pakistan’s energy crisis.
                              • It was a major step in regional energy cooperation.

                              Last verified on 29-04-2026

                              184

                              Which of the following bodies deal with charge in oil pricing mechanism in Pakistan?

                              Correct Answer: OGRA
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Oil and Gas Regulatory Authority (OGRA) regulates oil and gas pricing in Pakistan.
                              • It reviews and recommends petroleum prices.
                              • OGRA ensures transparency and fair pricing mechanism.
                              • It works under the Government of Pakistan.

                              Last verified on 29-04-2026

                              185

                              Article 35 A was abrogated by Modi Government on 5th August 2019 to indulge in?

                              Correct Answer: Demographic engineering
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Article 35A was revoked on 5 August 2019 by the Government of India.
                              • The move was widely viewed as enabling demographic changes in Kashmir.
                              • It removed special rights related to residency and property ownership.
                              • The decision was linked with revocation of Article 370.
                              • It created significant political and regional tensions.

                              Last verified on 29-04-2026

                              186

                              An agreement on the demarcation of boundaries was concluded between Pakistan and China in the year?

                              Correct Answer: 1963
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • An agreement on the demarcation of boundaries was concluded between Pakistan and China in the year on 2 March 1963.
                              • Pak china's boundary line was demarcated during the era of Ayyub Khan.
                              • This agreement called The Sino-Pakistan Agreement.
                              • The Sino-Pakistan Agreement is a 1963 document between the governments of Pakistan and China establishing the border between those countries in the disputed Kashmir region.

                              The agreement was signed by: 

                              • China: Marshal Chen Yi, Plenipotentiary of the Government of the People's Republic of China
                              • Pakistan: Z. Ali Bhutto, Plenipotentiary of the Government of Pakistan

                              Last verified on 30-03-2026

                              187

                              The plains of 2900 Km long River Indus make up ____ of total land of Pakistan.

                              Correct Answer: 20%
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The plains of the Indus River are very fertile and extensive.
                              • They cover about 20% of Pakistan’s total land area.
                              • The Indus River is approximately 2900 km long.
                              • These plains are important for agriculture and population settlement.
                              • They form the backbone of Pakistan’s irrigation system.

                              Last verified on 29-04-2026

                              188

                              Hindu became furious and Congress led by _____ swung into action to oppose the decision of Bengal’s partition.

                              Correct Answer: Sir Pheroze Shah Mehta
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The opposition to the Partition of Bengal (1905) was led by Congress leaders.
                              • Sir Pherozeshah Mehta was an important early Congress leader.
                              • He played a key role in mobilizing opposition against British decision.
                              • The movement led to the Swadeshi and Boycott Movement.
                              • It became a major phase of Indian nationalist struggle.

                              Last verified on 29-04-2026

                              189

                              Pakistan is the Twentieth Century: A Political History is written by?

                              Correct Answer: Lawerence Ziring
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The book Pakistan in the Twentieth Century: A Political History is written by Lawrence Ziring.
                                • It presents a detailed political history of Pakistan.
                                • The book covers events from British India to modern Pakistan.

                                Additional information:

                                • Ayesha Jalal → The Sole Spokesman
                                • Sugata Bose → Modern South Asia
                                • Stanley Wolpert → Jinnah of Pakistan
                                • I. H. Qureshi → The Struggle for Pakistan

                                Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                190

                                Under the system of diarchy?

                                Correct Answer: Both A and B
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Dyarchy was introduced under the Government of India Act 1919 (Montagu–Chelmsford Reforms).

                                In this system, provincial powers were divided into two parts.

                                1. Some subjects were controlled by provincial ministers (elected representatives).
                                2. Other subjects were controlled by Governor and executive officers.

                                Transferred Subjects (Indian Ministers):

                                • Education
                                • Health
                                • Agriculture
                                • Local Government

                                Reserved Subjects (Governor):

                                • Police
                                • Finance
                                • Irrigation
                                • Law and Order

                                Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                191

                                Congress organized _____ as powerful tactic in their campaign against the Curzon administration (1899-1905).

                                Correct Answer: Boycott of British goods
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • During Lord Curzon (1899–1905), Congress used boycott of British goods as a protest tactic.
                                • It was part of the broader Swadeshi and anti-partition movement.
                                • The aim was to weaken British economic control.
                                • People were encouraged to use local Indian products instead of British goods.
                                • The boycott movement strengthened Indian nationalism.
                                • It laid the foundation for later mass movements like Non-Cooperation Movement (1920).

                                Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                192

                                What was the reaction of Congress in response to the failure of Cripps Plan?

                                Correct Answer: Quit India resolution
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • After the failure of the Cripps Mission, Congress reacted strongly.
                                • It launched the Quit India Movement in 1942.
                                • This was led by Mahatma Gandhi.
                                • The demand was for immediate British withdrawal from India.
                                • It became a major mass movement against British rule.

                                Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                193

                                Which of the following can help diversity Pakistan’s export basket?

                                Correct Answer: SEZs
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Special Economic Zones (SEZs) help diversify Pakistan’s export base.
                                • SEZs promote industrial growth and manufacturing.
                                • They attract local and foreign investment.
                                • This increases production of different export goods.
                                • As a result, Pakistan’s export basket becomes more diverse.

                                Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                194

                                The Tarbela dam is located on River Indus about 47 km upstream from?

                                Correct Answer: Attock
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Tarbela Dam is built on the River Indus.
                                • It is located about 47 km upstream from Attock.
                                • Situated in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.
                                • Completed in 1976 as a major water and power project.
                                • It is one of the largest earth-filled dams in the world.
                                • Reservoir area → ~250 km² (Tarbela Lake)
                                • Installed capacity → 4,888 MW
                                • Planned expansion → 6,418 MW (after extension)

                                Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                195

                                Which of the following were reformed by Sher Shah Suri?

                                Correct Answer: Both A and B
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Sher Shah Suri (1540–1545) introduced major reforms in India.
                                • He reformed the currency system (coinage system).
                                • He also improved police, judicial, and military administration.
                                • His reforms made governance more efficient and organized.

                                Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                196

                                Pakistan has more than 780,000 hectares of?

                                Correct Answer: Wetlands
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Pakistan has more than 780,000 hectares of wetlands.
                                • Wetlands include lakes, marshes, and river areas.
                                • They are important for biodiversity and migration of birds.
                                • Major wetlands are found in Sindh, Punjab, and coastal areas.

                                Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                197

                                Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah qualified as a Barrister in?

                                Correct Answer: 1895
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Muhammad Ali Jinnah qualified as a Barrister in 1895.
                                • He studied law in London at Lincoln’s Inn.
                                • After qualification, he returned to India to start his legal career.
                                • He became one of the most successful lawyers in Bombay.

                                Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                198

                                Which of the following are not Kharif crops? Barely and Mustard, Rice and Sugarcane, Cotton and Maize, Maize and Millet

                                Correct Answer: Barely and Mustard
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Agriculture divides crops into Kharif (summer) and Rabi (winter).
                                  • Barley and Mustard are Rabi crops.
                                  • Kharif crops are grown in summer season (rainy season).
                                  • Barley and mustard grow in winter season (cool climate).

                                  Kharif Crops (Summer)

                                  • Rice
                                  • Cotton
                                  • Maize
                                  • Sugarcane
                                  • Millet

                                  Rabi Crops (Winter)

                                  • Wheat
                                  • Barley
                                  • Mustard
                                  • Gram (Chickpea)
                                  • Lentils

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  199

                                  More than _______ of Pakistan’s forests are coniferous and scrub forest.

                                  Correct Answer: 36%
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • More than 36% of Pakistan’s forests are coniferous and scrub forests.
                                  • These forests are mostly found in northern and hilly areas.
                                  • Coniferous forests include pine, deodar, and fir trees.
                                  • Scrub forests grow in dry and semi-arid regions.

                                  Additional information:

                                  • Riverine forests → ~7%
                                  • Mangrove forests → ~7%
                                  • Irrigated plantations → ~10%

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  200

                                  Sukkur Barrage was built in which year?

                                  Correct Answer: 1932
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • The Sukkur Barrage, officially named Lloyd Barrage, is located in Sindh Province.
                                  • It built from 1923 to 1932 during the British Raj.
                                  • It was rehabilitated in November 2004. 
                                  • The barrage is approximately 2 km (1 mi) long.
                                  • It is the oldest in Pakistan, with seven canals taking off from it.

                                  Last verified on 14-03-2026

                                  201

                                  The Government of Hari Enquiry Committee (1947-48) recommended the abolition of?

                                  Correct Answer: Begar (free labour)
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • The Hari Enquiry Committee was formed to review agricultural conditions.
                                  • It recommended abolition of Begar (forced/free labour system).
                                  • Begar meant farmers were forced to work without wages.
                                  • The committee aimed to improve rights of peasants (Haris).
                                  • It was an important step toward agricultural reforms in Sindh.

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  202

                                  Bieter became the ruler of Ferghana, a state which was in?

                                  Correct Answer: Central Asia
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Ferghana (Fergana Valley) is located in Central Asia.
                                  • It was ruled by Babur in his early life.
                                  • Babur became ruler of Ferghana at a young age in 1494 AD.
                                  • The region is now part of modern Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan, and Tajikistan.
                                  • It later became the starting point of the Mughal Empire expansion.

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  203

                                  "The Hindu settlement, the Hindu attitude to the minorities and the Hindu renaissance that there was no hope of unity" - Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah said after the end of?

                                  Correct Answer: Second Round Table Conference
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • This statement was made by Muhammad Ali Jinnah after the Second Round Table Conference (1931).
                                  • He expressed disappointment over Hindu political attitudes toward minorities.
                                  • The conference failed to resolve constitutional issues in India.
                                  • It increased Muslim–Hindu political differences.
                                  • This strengthened Jinnah’s belief in a separate Muslim identity.

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  204

                                  According to ________ Article of the 1973 Constitution the validity of the proceedings in Parliament shall not called in question on the ground of any irregularity of procedure in any court of law.

                                  Correct Answer: Article 69
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Constitution of Pakistan 1973 Article 69 protects parliamentary proceedings.
                                  • It states that court cannot question Parliament’s proceedings on procedural irregularities.
                                  • This ensures parliamentary independence.
                                  • Courts can only intervene in cases of constitutional violation, not procedure issues.

                                  Additional information:

                                  • Article 68 → Restrictions on discussion in Parliament
                                  • Article 70 → Introduction of bills
                                  • Article 85 → Federal Consolidated Fund matters
                                  • Article 86 → Supplementary budget provisions

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  205

                                  Which of the following was/were introduced by British in India?

                                  Correct Answer: All of these
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • The British introduced major modern systems in India during their rule.
                                  • They established the telegraph system for fast communication.
                                  • They built the railway network for transport and trade.
                                  • They also influenced society with British values, laws, and norms.
                                  • 1853 → First railway line (Bombay to Thane)
                                  • 1850s → Telegraph system introduced
                                  • 1860s → Postal system expanded

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  206

                                  The disobedience organized by Congress to be discontinued under?

                                  Correct Answer: Gandhi-Irwin Pact
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • The civil disobedience movement led by the Congress was suspended under the Gandhi-Irwin Pact.
                                  • It was signed between Mahatma Gandhi and Lord Irwin in 1931.
                                  • Congress agreed to stop the Civil Disobedience Movement.
                                  • In return, political prisoners were released and restrictions were eased.

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  207

                                  Sindh was constituted as an independent province on?

                                  Correct Answer: 1st April 1936
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Sindh was separated from the Bombay Presidency on 1st April 1936.
                                  • It was given the status of an independent province under British rule.
                                  • The capital of Sindh was established in Karachi.
                                  • This reform was part of the Government of India Act 1935.

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  208

                                  Pakistan successfully conducted flight test of ________ weapon system in October 2023.

                                  Correct Answer: Ababeel
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Pakistan conducted a successful test of the Ababeel missile system in October 2023.
                                  • According to Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR), the test was successful.
                                  • Ababeel is a multiple independently targetable reentry vehicle (MIRV) missile.
                                  • Its name means “Flock of Birds” in Arabic.

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  209

                                  Pakistan has coastline of about?

                                  Correct Answer: More than 1000 Km
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Pakistan has a coastline along the Arabian Sea.
                                  • The total length is more than 1000 km.
                                  • Sindh coast → Karachi, Thatta, Badin
                                  • Balochistan coast → Gwadar, Pasni, Ormara
                                  • Major port → Karachi Port
                                  • Deep sea port → Gwadar Port

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  210

                                  Who wrote the pamphlet on “The separation of Sindh from Bombay” in 1924?

                                  Correct Answer: Abdul Majeed Sindhi
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • The pamphlet on separation of Sindh from Bombay Presidency was written in 1924.
                                  • It was authored by G. M. Bhurgri.
                                  • He strongly supported the administrative separation of Sindh.
                                  • The demand was part of early Sindhi political movements.
                                  • It later contributed to the final separation in 1936.

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  211

                                  The cold desert named Katpana Desert is situated in which country?

                                  Correct Answer: Pakistan
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Katpana Desert is a cold desert located in Skardu, Gilgit-Baltistan, Pakistan.
                                  • Popular for photography, jeep safaris, and desert snow landscapes.

                                  Additional information:

                                  • Altitude: 2,226 meters above sea level
                                  • Global ranking: 16th largest desert in the world
                                  • Approximate area: ~50 km²
                                  • Average winter temperature: -10°C to 5°C

                                  Last verified on 12-03-2026

                                  212

                                  Article 3 of the Constitution of 1973 provides for?

                                  Correct Answer: Elimination of exploitation
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Constitution of Pakistan 1973 Article 3 focuses on elimination of exploitation.
                                  • It ensures that no individual or class exploits another.
                                  • Aims to establish social justice and equality.

                                  Additional information:

                                  • Article 2 → Islam as State Religion
                                  • Article 4 → Right of individuals to be dealt with in accordance with law
                                  • Article 25 → Equality of citizens
                                  • Article 36 → Protection of minorities

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  213

                                  A “History of the people of Pakistan” is written by?

                                  Correct Answer: J. Hussain
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • A History of the People of Pakistan is written by J. Hussain.
                                  • The book explains the historical development of Muslims in the subcontinent.
                                  • It covers political, cultural, and social aspects of Pakistan.

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  214

                                  Who was the Governor General of India during the conquest of Sindh?

                                  Correct Answer: Lord Ellenborough
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Lord Ellenborough was Governor-General during the conquest of Sindh (1843).
                                  • He served from 1842 to 1844.
                                  • The conquest was carried out by Charles Napier.
                                  • Sindh was annexed into British India after this campaign.

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  215

                                  The idea of separation of Sindh from Bombay was first presented in?

                                  Correct Answer: 1913
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • The idea of separating Sindh from Bombay Presidency was first presented in 1913.
                                  • It was raised by Sindhi political leaders including G. M. Syed.
                                  • The demand aimed to give Sindh administrative independence.
                                  • It later gained support from the All India Muslim League.
                                  • Sindh was finally separated in 1936.

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  216

                                  "Shahnama" was written by _____?

                                  Correct Answer: Firdausi
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • "Shahnama" is an epic poem that was written in 1010.
                                  • It contains about 60,000 couplets.
                                  • The author of "Shahnama" was Abu Al-Qasim Firdausi.
                                  • The epic poem is also known as the "Book of Kings".

                                  Last verified on 30-03-2026

                                  217

                                  In _____ the East India Company began to fortify their important ac Calcutta.

                                  Correct Answer: 1750s
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • The East India Company started fortifying its base in Calcutta during the 1750s.
                                  • This was due to rising tensions with Siraj-ud-Daulah.
                                  • The fortification led to conflict between the British and Bengal rulers.
                                  • It became one of the causes of the Battle of Plassey (1857).

                                  Last verified on 29-04-2026

                                  218

                                  The Kashaf-ul-Mahjub was written by?

                                  Correct Answer: Hazrat Data Gunj Buksh/Hazrat Ali Hajveri
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Kashf al-Mahjub was written by Ali Hujwiri.
                                  • He is also known as Data Ganj Bakhsh (RA).
                                  • The book is one of the earliest works on Sufism in Persian.
                                  • Birth: ~1009 AD (Ghazni)
                                  • Death: 8 August 1072 (Lahore)

                                  Last verified on 31-03-2026
                                  219

                                  Millat of Islam is written by whom?

                                  Correct Answer: Chaudhry Rehmat Ali
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • "The Millat of Islam and the Menace of Indianism" was written by Chaudhry Rehmat Ali
                                  • Published in the early 1940s (often cited as 1940-1942).

                                  Additional information 

                                  • He was a key figure in the Pakistan National Movement 
                                  • He is best known for coining the term "Pakistan". 

                                  Last updated by NA on 28April 2026


                                  220

                                  Term and tenure of Senate membership are _____ years in Pakistan.

                                  Correct Answer: Six years
