                                  221

                                  When Muhammad Ali jinnah was given the title of Quaid-e-Azam

                                  Correct Answer: 1938
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • The title “Quaid-e-Azam” was given in 1938
                                  • The title was given by Maulana Mazharuddin Shaheed 
                                  • Pakistan's first governor-general until his death
                                  • Leader of All-India Muslim League (1913–1947)
                                  • Founder of Pakistan (14 August 1947)
                                  • First Governor-General of Pakistan (1947–48)
                                  • Born: 25 December 1876 (Karachi)
                                  • Died: 11 September 1948 (Karachi)
                                  • Spouse: Rattanbai Jinnah, Emibai Jinnah
                                  • Daughter: Dina Wadia
                                  • Sister: Fatima Jinnah

                                  Last updated by NA on 27 April 2026



                                  222

                                  Smallest dam in Pakistan is?

                                  Correct Answer: Warsak dam
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Warsak Dam is the smallest major dam in Pakistan.
                                  • Located on the Kabul River in Peshawar,
                                  • Built on the Kabul River
                                  • Construction began in 1949
                                  • Opened: 1960
                                  • Height: 76 m
                                  • Length: 140.2 m (460 ft)
                                  • Completed under the Colombo Plan in two phases and financed by the Canadian Government. 


                                  Additional information

                                  The largest dam of Pakistan is the Tarbela Dam

                                  Last updated by NA on 27 April 2026

                                  223

                                  F-16 jet used by Pakistan are manufactured by ?

                                  Correct Answer: USA
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • The F-16 Fighting Falcon jets operated by the Pakistan Air Force (PAF) 
                                  • Currently, Pakistan operates Block 70 or F-16 Viper configuration.
                                  • Manufacturer: Lockheed Martin (originally General Dynamics).
                                  • Production was taken over by Lockheed in the 1990s.
                                  • Developed by General Dynamics
                                  • Origin: United States.

                                  Last updated by NA on 27 April 2026

                                  224

                                  Which Pakistani province has the "largest proven natural gas reserves?

                                  Correct Answer: Balochistan
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Balochistan has the largest proven natural gas reserves in Pakistan.
                                  • The famous Sui Gas Field (discovered in 1952) is located here.
                                  • 35% Reserves = Balochistan (largest).
                                  • 60 - 70% Production = Sindh (highest).

                                  Last verified on 27-04-2026

                                  225

                                  The Constitution of 1962 gave more power to the?

                                  Correct Answer: The President
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Constitution year: 1962
                                  • System: Presidential system
                                  • Key power holder: President
                                  • Era: Ayub Khan period
                                  • Type: Second Constitution of Pakistan

                                  Last verified on 27-04-2026

                                  226

                                  Kitab-ul-Umm was written by ______?

                                  Correct Answer: Imam Shafi
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Kitab-ul-Umm was written by Imam Shafi’i, a renowned Islamic jurist.
                                  • It is considered the first major book of Fiqh.
                                  • The book explains Islamic laws and jurisprudence in detail.
                                  • Its teachings form the basis of the Shafi’i school of thought.
                                  • Published between 1903–1907 in printed form.

                                  Last verified on 01-04-2026

                                  227

                                  Tarbela Dam is located in ____ district of Khyber Pakhtuankhwah.

                                  Correct Answer: Haripur
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Tarbela Dam is located in the Haripur District (and partially extends into the Swabi District) 
                                  • was built on the Indus River in KPk.
                                  • It is located mainly in the Swabi District Tehsil Topi of KPK.
                                  • It is the largest earth-filled dam in the world
                                  • The Construction began in 1968  
                                  • They were Opened in 1976
                                  • Height: 470 feet (143 m)

                                  Last question by NA on 26 April 2026



                                  228

                                  Simla agreement was signed between Indira Gandhi and ________?

                                  Correct Answer: Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • The Shimla agreement between:
                                    • Prime Minister Indira Gandhi from India, and President Z. A. Bhutto from Pakistan.
                                  • This agreement followed the Indo-Pakistani War of 1971
                                  • It was signed on 2 July 1972 in Shimla, India.

                                  Last question by NA on 26 April 2026

                                  229

                                  The Punjab irrigation system is part of the Indus Basin system with main canal commands off-taking from how many barrages over the Indus and its tributaries?

                                  Correct Answer: 12
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • The Punjab irrigation system is part of the Indus Basin,
                                  Punjab Irrigation Department manages the following barrages:

                                    1. Jinnah Barrage on River Induu
                                    2. Taunsa Barrage on River Indus
                                    3. Rasul Barrage on River Jhelum
                                    4. Marala Headworks on River Chenab
                                    5. Qadirabad Barrage on River Chenab
                                    6. New Khanki Barrage River Chenab
                                    7. Balloki Barrage on River Ravi
                                    8. Trimmu Barrage on River Chenab
                                    9. Sidhnai Barrage on River Ravi
                                    10. Islam Barrage on River Sutlej
                                    11. Suleimanki Barrage on River Sutlej
                                    12. Panjnad Barrage on River Indus
                                    Additional information

                                      • Located on the Indus River and its tributaries (Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, and Sutlej)
                                      • It comprises 3 major reservoirs (Tarbela, Mangla, Chashma)
                                      • Punjab Irrigation Department Headquarters in Lahore
                                      • One of the largest irrigation systems in the world
                                      • Supports agriculture in Southern Punjab.

                                      Last question by NA on 26 April 2026

                                      230

                                      Tarbela Dam is located in which province of Pakistan?

                                      Correct Answer: KPK
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation
                                      • Tarbela Dam is located in the Haripur district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.
                                      • The largest earth-filled dam in the world
                                      • It is 485 feet (148 m) high above the Indus riverbed.
                                      • Its construction began in 1968
                                      • It was completed in 1976
                                      • Construction was undertaken by the Italian firm Salini Impregilo.

                                      Last question by NA on 26 April 2026

                                      231

                                      The boundary between Pakistan and India is known as ____?

                                      Correct Answer: Redcliff Line
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation
                                      • The boundary between Pakistan and India is known as the Radcliffe Line.
                                      • They separate India from Pakistan and Bangladesh.
                                      • It was named after Sir Cyril Radcliffe
                                      • Established on August 17, 1947. 
                                      • Length: 3323 km

                                      Additional information 

                                      • Agreement Between:
                                        • Sir Cyril Radcliffe* (Chairman of the Boundary Commission)
                                        • Leaders of British India, the Indian National Congress, and the All-India Muslim League

                                      Last question by NA on 26 April 2026



                                      232

                                      The Viceroy who put forward the proposal of August offer in 1940?

                                      Correct Answer: Lord Linlithgow
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation
                                      • The famous August Offer was made by the Viceroy named Lord Linlithgow
                                      • Victor Alexander John Hope, 2nd Marquess of Linlithgow
                                      • Purpose: To secure Indian cooperation in World War II.
                                      • Date: August 8, 1940.


                                      Last question by NA on 26 April 2026

                                      233

                                      The Lord Linlithgow put forward the proposal of August Offer in _____?

                                      Correct Answer: 1940
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation
                                      • Lord Linlithgow put forward the August Offer on August 8, 1940,
                                      • Aiming to secure Indian cooperation in World War II
                                      • On 8 August 1940, early in the Battle of Britain,
                                      • The Viceroy of India, Lord Linlithgow, made the so-called "August Offer" at Simla.


                                      Last question by NA on 26 April 2026

                                      234

                                      Great personalities like Allama Muhammad Iqbal are born in centuries. His first public appearance was in?

                                      Correct Answer: 1900
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation
                                      • Allama Muhammad Iqbal's first public appearance was in 1900.
                                      • At the annual session of the Anjuman-e-Himayat-e-Islam in Lahore.
                                      • During, he recited his poem "Nala-e-Yateem,"  (Wails of an Orphan)

                                      Additional information 

                                      Allama Iqbal

                                      • Born: November 9, 1877 (Sialkot)
                                      • Father: Sheikh Noor Muhammad
                                      • Mother: Imam Bibi
                                      • Received PhD: Germany
                                      • Title: Poet of the East
                                      • Famous books: Bang-e-Dara, Zarb-i-Kalim, Armughan-e-Hijaz
                                      • Famous Punjabi Books/Poem: Piyaara Jedi, Baba Bakri Wala
                                      • Died: 21 April 1938
                                      • Burial: Badshahi Mosque in Lahore

                                      Last question by NA on 26 April 2026

                                      235

                                      Allama Muhammad Iqbal, first public appearance was in 1900 at the annual session of?

                                      Correct Answer: Anjuman-e-Himayat-e-Islam
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation
                                      • On February 24, 1900 Allama Muhammad Iqbal's first major public appearance
                                        • At the Anjuman-e-Himayat-e-Islam in Lahore.
                                      • During, he recited his poem "Nala-e-Yateem,"  (Wails of an Orphan)

                                      Additional information 

                                      Allama Iqbal

                                      • Born: November 9, 1877 (Sialkot)
                                      • Received PhD: Germany
                                      • Title: Poet of the East
                                      • Died: 21 April 1938
                                      • Burial: Badshahi Mosque in Lahore

                                      Last question by NA on 26 April 2026

                                      236

                                      Which two subjects did Sindh give to the Federal Government?

                                      Correct Answer: Defense and Foreign Affairs
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation
                                      • Sindh gave Defense and Foreign Affairs to the Federal Government.
                                      • These subjects are managed at the national level.
                                      • It helps in strong central control of security.
                                      • Ensures unity and national stability.

                                      Last updated by NA on 26 April 2026

                                      237

                                      How was the Chief of Army Staff at the time of Kargil in 1999?

                                      Correct Answer: General Parvez Musharaf
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation
                                      •  Pakistan COAS during Kargil War: Gen. Pervez Musharraf
                                      • India COAS: General V. P. Malik
                                      • Captain Karnal Sher Khan Shaheed was the hero of the war.

                                      Additional information

                                      • Kargil War was fought between India and Pakistan.
                                      • It took place from May to July 1999.
                                      • It is also known as the Kargil Conflict / Operation Vijay (India).
                                      • Location was the Kargil district along the Line of Control (LoC).
                                      • The conflict was stopped with the USA's intervention.
                                      • India had about 30,000 troops, Pakistan had about 5,000.
                                      • India won the Kargil War
                                      • India lost 527 soldiers
                                      • Pakistan lost 453 soldiers.

                                      Last updated by NA on 26 April 2026

                                      238

                                      The Kargil incident happened in which year?

                                      Correct Answer: 1999
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation
                                      • Kargil War was fought between India and Pakistan.
                                      • It took place from May to July 1999.
                                      • It is also known as the Kargil Conflict / Operation Vijay (India).
                                      • Location was the Kargil district along the Line of Control (LoC).

                                      Additional information

                                      • The conflict was stopped with the USA's intervention.
                                      • India had about 30,000 troops, Pakistan had about 5,000.
                                      • Captain Karnal Sher Khan Shaheed was the hero of the war.
                                      • General Pervez Musharraf was Pakistan’s COAS.
                                        • General V. P. Malik was India’s COAS.
                                        • India won the Kargil War
                                        • India lost 527 soldiers
                                        • Pakistan lost 453 soldiers.


                                        Last updated by NA on 26 April 2026

                                        239

                                        Sir Syed Ahmad Khan established MAO to counter political hegemony of ?

                                        Correct Answer: Hindu-dominated Congress
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Sir Syed Ahmad Khan founded MAO College on May 24, 1875 (as a school).
                                        • To counter Hindu political dominance.
                                        • Prepared Muslims for government jobs.

                                        Additional information

                                        • Location: Aligarh, Uttar Pradesh.
                                        • Became a college two years later (1877)
                                        • Known initially as Madarsatul Uloom
                                        • Inspired by the Cambridge education system,
                                        • Began operations on Queen Victoria's 56th birthday
                                        • Intermediate classes were introduced in 1878.
                                        • B.A. degree classes in 1881
                                        • Aligarh Muslim University in 1920


                                        Last updated question by NA 22-April-2026

                                        240

                                        When Radcliffe Award was announced?

                                        Correct Answer: 17th August 1947
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • The Radcliffe Award was published on August 17, 1947.
                                        • It was the result of the partition plan
                                        • Announced by Lord Mountbatten 
                                        • It outlined the boundary division between India and Pakistan.
                                        • It divided Punjab and Bengal based on religious majorities
                                        • It was named after Sir Cyril Radcliffe, who chaired the boundary commission.

                                        Last question by NA on 26 April 2026

                                        241

                                        In April 2026, Pakistan launched an indigenous satellite from which country?

                                        Correct Answer: China
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation

                                        On 25 April 2026, The Pakistan Space and Upper Atmosphere Res­earch Commission (Sup­arco) successfully launched Pakistan’s indigenous Electro-Optical Satellite (EO-3) from China’s Taiyuan Satellite Launch Centre.

                                         

                                        Additional Information:

                                        Equipped with advanced imaging capabilities, the EO-3 satellite is expected to play a vital role in transforming urban planning, disaster management, food security and environmental protection across Pakistan.

                                        ***

                                        242

                                        Mangroves Forests are at?

                                        Correct Answer: Karachi
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Mangrove forests in Pakistan are mainly found near Karachi.
                                        • They are located in the Indus Delta along the Arabian Sea.
                                        • These forests grow in salty coastal water.
                                        • They protect coastlines from erosion and storms.

                                        Last verified on 26-04-2026

                                        243

                                        Wind Corridors for energy are in?

                                        Correct Answer: Jhampir
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • The main wind energy corridor in Pakistan is located in Jhampir, Sindh.
                                        • Jhampir is part of the Gharo–Jhampir Wind Corridor, one of Asia’s largest wind belts.
                                        • It lies between Karachi and Hyderabad.
                                        • This area has strong and consistent winds ideal for power generation.
                                        • Many wind power projects are installed here.

                                        Last verified on 26-04-2026

                                        244

                                        UNESCO declared Mohen jo Daro as a World heritage site in the year?

                                        Correct Answer: 1980
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Mohenjo-daro was declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO in 1980.
                                        • It is one of the most important sites of the Indus Valley Civilization.
                                        • The city dates back to around 2500 BC.
                                        • It shows advanced urban planning and drainage systems.
                                        • It is located in Larkana, Sindh, Pakistan.
                                        • Discovered in 1922 by R. D. Banerji.

                                        Last verified on 26-04-2026

                                        245

                                        Who had ordered Hazrat Ali Hijwary (R.A) to come to Lahore for preaching?

                                        Correct Answer: Hazrat Sheikh Abu al-Fazl
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Hazrat Ali Hujwiri (RA), also known as Data Ganj Bakhsh, came to Lahore for preaching Islam.
                                        • He was directed by his spiritual guide Sheikh Abu al-Fazl.
                                        • He settled in Lahore and spread Sufism and Islamic teachings.

                                        Last verified on 26-04-2026

                                        246

                                        What is Kashmir in relation to Pakistan?

                                        Correct Answer: A disputed region
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • AJK has been the subject of a dispute between India and Pakistan since 1947 
                                          • Pakistan controls parts known as Azad Jammu & Kashmir and Gilgit-Baltistan.
                                          •  India controls the regions of Jammu, Kashmir Valley, and Ladakh.
                                        • Government: Type Self-governing state under Pakistani administration


                                        Additional information

                                        • The Capital of AJK is Muzaffarabad
                                        • The largest city is Muzaffarabad
                                        • Total Districts of AJK: 10
                                        • Total Divisions of AJK: 3
                                        • Total Tehsils of AJK: 33
                                        • Team: AJK Jaguars

                                        247

                                        Who was the first emperor and founder of the Ottoman Empire?

                                        Correct Answer: Osman I
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Osman I was the founder of the Ottoman Empire.
                                        • He established the empire in 1299 AD.
                                        • He was a Turkish tribal leader known as Osman Gazi.
                                        • His reign lasted from 1299 to 1324 AD.

                                        Last verified on 26-04-2026

                                        248

                                        Give the name of the author of "The Meaning of Pakistan"?

                                        Correct Answer: F.K.K. Durrani
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • The Meaning of Pakistan is a book about Pakistan’s ideology and history.
                                        • It explains the concept and importance of Pakistan.
                                        • The author is F.K.K. Durrani.
                                        • The book was published in 1944.

                                        Last verified on 26-04-2026

                                        249

                                        Indus River system in Pakistan comprises of?

                                        Correct Answer: Five rivers
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • The Indus River system is mainly known as the Punjab river system.
                                        • It consists of five major rivers.
                                        • These rivers are: Indus, Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, and Sutlej.
                                        • They are the main source of irrigation in Pakistan.
                                        • This system supports agriculture and economy.

                                        Last verified on 26-04-2026

                                        250

                                        The Quit India Movement, or the India August Movement, was launched in _____?

                                        Correct Answer: 1942
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • The Quit India Movement is also known as the August Movement.
                                        • It was launched by Gandhi on August 8, 1942, in Bombay.
                                        • The movement, started by the Congress party.
                                        • It's demanded an end to British rule in India.
                                        • Muhammad Ali Jinnah responded with the slogan "Divide and Quit India."

                                        Last verified on 26-04-2026

                                        251

                                        What was the response of Muslim league over British offer of 1940?

                                        Correct Answer: Neither accepted nor rejected
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • In 1940, the British made certain constitutional proposals for India.
                                        • The Muslim League did not fully accept or reject them.
                                        • Instead, it focused on the demand for a separate Muslim state.
                                        • This was the time when the Lahore Resolution (23 March 1940) was passed.
                                        • The League’s main goal became the creation of Pakistan.

                                        Last verified on 26-04-2026

                                        252

                                        "Tadhkirah Sufia-e-Sindh" is a famous book of Mr.?

                                        Correct Answer: Ijaz-ul-Haqq Quddoosi
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Tadhkirah Sufia-e-Sindh is written by Ijaz-ul-Haqq Quddoosi.
                                        • First Published: 1959
                                        • Publisher: Urdu Academy Sindh, Karachi
                                        • Language: Urdu
                                        • Region Covered: Sindh
                                        • Type: Biographical (Sufi Saints)

                                        Last verified on 26-04-2026

                                        253

                                        Which one of the following Muslim leaders was expelled from the Muslim League by the Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah?

                                        Correct Answer: Fazlul Haq
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • A. K. Fazlul Haq was expelled from the All-India Muslim League by Muhammad Ali Jinnah.
                                        • This happened in 1941 due to political differences.
                                        • Fazlul Haq had earlier presented the Lahore Resolution (1940).
                                        • Later, he formed alliances that went against Muslim League policies.
                                        • This led to his removal from the party.

                                        Last verified on 25-04-2026

                                        254

                                        ‘Chotiari Reservoir’ is situated in which province?

                                        Correct Answer: Sindh
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Chotiari Dam is an artificial water reservoir.
                                        • It situated 35 km away from the Sanghar town in the Sanghar District of the Sindh province of Pakistan.
                                        • Chotiari Dam is another name for Baqar Lake.
                                        • It is also known as the "Jungle of Lakes".
                                        • The reservoir is fed by the Nara Canal.

                                        Last verified on 25-04-2026

                                        255

                                        Largest Granite rock deposits in Pakistan are located at?

                                        Correct Answer: Karoonjhar hills in Sindh
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • The largest granite deposits in Pakistan are found in the Karoonjhar Hills (Sindh).
                                        • These hills are located in the Tharparkar region.

                                        Additional information:

                                        • Chagai Mountains → Gold & copper
                                        • Saindak → Copper & gold
                                        • Salt Range → Rock salt
                                        • Dera Ghazi Khan → Uranium & minerals

                                        Last verified on 25-04-2026

                                        256

                                        Who gave the idea of 'Divine affirmation of God's Uniqueness' (Tauheed-i-Illah)?

                                        Correct Answer: Akbar
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Founder → Akbar
                                        • Year → 1582 AD
                                        • Name → Din-i-Ilahi / Tauheed-i-Illahi
                                        • Nature → Syncretic (mixed religious system)

                                        Correct Answer: Syed Ameer Ali

                                        • It was published in 1891.
                                        • The book provides a comprehensive study of Islam's core principles and beliefs.

                                        Last verified on 25-04-2026

                                        258

                                        Hijjaj Bin Yousuf was the Governor of Iran.

                                        Correct Answer: False
                                        Show explanation

                                        • He was mainly appointed as the Governor of Iraq and its eastern regions, not specifically Iran.

                                        Last verified on 25-04-2026

                                        259

                                        1st battle of Panipat was fought in?

                                        Correct Answer: 1526
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • The First Battle of Panipat was fought between Babur and the Lodi Empire,
                                        • Took place on 21 April 1526 in North India.
                                        • Result: Mughal victory
                                        • Territorial changes: The Delhi Sultanate was annexed by the Mughal dynasty
                                        • 2nd battle of Panipat: 1556
                                        • 3rd battle of Panipat: 1761

                                        260

                                        Which is the city of Shrines and Tombs?

                                        Correct Answer: Multan
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Multan is a city in the Punjab province
                                        • Known as the "City of Saints" and the "City of Shrines and Tombs."
                                        • Located along the eastern bank of the Chenab River
                                        • It is the sixth-largest city
                                        • Settled: 3000 BC
                                        • Government Type: Metropolitan Corporation

                                        Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026

                                        261

                                        Which is the third largest city of Pakistan?

                                        Correct Answer: Faisalabad
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation

                                        • Faisalabad is the third-largest city in Pakistan by population
                                        • Settled: 1890
                                        • Former name: Lyallpur
                                        • Founded by: James Broadwood Lyall
                                        • Named after: Faisal of Saudi Arabia


                                        Here is a list of the ten largest cities in Pakistan by population:

                                        1. Karachi
                                        2. Lahore
                                        3. Faisalabad
                                        4. Rawalpindi
                                        5. Gujranwala
                                        6. Multan
                                        7. Hyderabad
                                        8. Peshawar
                                        9. Islamabad (Capital city)
                                        10. Quetta

                                        *****

                                        Last updated by NS on 06 May 2026

                                        262

                                        Battle of Plassey was fought in ______?

                                        Correct Answer: 1757
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Fought between: British East India Company (led by Robert Clive) vs. Nawab of Bengal (Siraj-ud-Daulah) and his French allies.
                                        • The Battle of Plassey was fought on 23 June 1757.
                                        • It established British dominance in India.
                                        • Location: Palashi (Plassey), Bengal.
                                        • Captured Calcutta
                                        Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026


                                        263

                                        "Shikwa" and "Jawab-e-Shikwa" are part of which poetry book?

                                        Correct Answer: Bang-e-Dara
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • "Shikwa" (Complaint) and "Jawab-e-Shikwa" (Answer to the Complaint)
                                        • Found in the book "Bang-e-Dara"  (The Call of the Caravan Bell).
                                        • Are iconic Urdu poems by Allama Muhammad Iqbal,
                                        • Originally published: 1924

                                        Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026

                                        264

                                        What is official cricket league of Pakistan?

                                        Correct Answer: Pakistan Super League
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • The official cricket league in Pakistan is the Pakistan Super League (PSL). 
                                        • Organised by the Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB).
                                        • Teams: 8 franchises (as of 2026),
                                        • Headquarters: Gaddafi Stadium
                                        • Format: Twenty20 (T20).
                                        • First edition: 2016
                                        • Latest edition: 2025
                                        • Next edition: 2026

                                        Additional information 

                                        Indian Premier League (IPL): Official league of India.

                                        Bangladesh Premier League (BPL): Official league of Bangladesh.

                                        Caribbean Premier League (CPL): Official league of the West Indies/Caribbean.


                                        Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026

                                        265

                                        Which is the former capital of Pakistan?

                                        Correct Answer: Karachi
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Karachi was the first capital of Pakistan 
                                        • From 1947 until 1959, for 12 years
                                        • Selected by Muhammad Ali Jinnah.
                                        • Interim Capital (1959–1967): Rawalpindi.
                                        • Current Capital ( August 14, 1967–Present): Islamabad. 

                                        Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026

                                        266

                                        In which Islamic month Pakistan was founded ?

                                        Correct Answer: Ramzan
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Pakistan was founded in the Islamic month of Ramadan
                                        • On 27th Ramadan 1366 AH on Friday
                                        • Gregorian Date: August 14, 1947

                                        Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026

                                        267

                                        Which city is called the Heart of KPK?

                                        Correct Answer: Peshawar
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Peshawar is considered the heart of KPK
                                        • The provincial capital, largest city
                                        • Known as the "City of Flowers"
                                        • Located in the north-west
                                        Additional information 
                                        Manchester of Pakistan. Faisalabad.

                                        Gateway of Pakistan. Karachi.

                                        Brasilia of Pakistan. Islamabad.

                                        City of Textiles. Faisalabad. 

                                        City of lightening. Karachi.


                                        Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026
                                        268

                                        The Pakistan and China economic project is called?

                                        Correct Answer: CPEC
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • The Pakistan-China economic project is called the CPEC.
                                        • CPEC stands for China-Pakistan Economic Corridor
                                        • Mission statement: Securing Energy Import and Trade Boost for China, Infrastructure Development for Pakistan
                                        • Established: 20 April 2015 
                                        • Budget: $62 billion

                                        Last updated by NA on 24-April-2026

                                        269

                                        Which organization provide financial assistance to countries like Pakistan?

                                        Correct Answer: IMF
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Major organizations providing financial assistance to Pakistan are the IMF
                                        • Pakistan officially became a member of the IMF on July 11, 1950.
                                        • IMF stands for International Monetary Fund.

                                        Last updated by NA on 23-April-2026

                                        270

                                        Pakistan conducted its first nuclear test in _____?

                                        Correct Answer: Chaghi
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation


                                        • Pakistan conducted its first nuclear tests, codenamed Chagai-I
                                        • Location: Ras Koh Hills, Chagai District, Balochistan.
                                        • Date: May 28, 1998 (Chagai-I).
                                        • Chagai-I was Pakistan's first public test of nuclear weapons.

                                        Additional information

                                        Chagai II

                                        Second atomic test conducted by Pakistan, carried out on 30 May 1998

                                        These tests followed the Chagai-I series

                                        The test site was Kharan Desert, Pakistan

                                        The number of tests is 1

                                        Last updated by NA on 24-April-2026

                                        271

                                        Ghauri missile of Pakistan was developed with the help of which country?

                                        Correct Answer: North Korea
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Pakistan's Ghauri-I (Hatf-V) missile was developed primarily with technical support from North Korea

                                        Production history

                                        • Designer: Khan Research Laboratories (KRL)
                                        • Designed: 1994–2001
                                        • Manufacturer: Khan Research Laboratories
                                        • No. built: 30 (estimated 2021)
                                        • Variants: Ghauri-II, Ghauri-III



                                        Last updated by NA on 23-April-2026

                                        272

                                        Which day is celebrated in Pakistan on 28th May?

                                        Correct Answer: Youm-e-Takbeer
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Youm-e-Takbi is celebrated as a national day in Pakistan on 28 May
                                        • In commemoration of Chagai-I and Chagai-II series of nuclear tests



                                        Last updated by NA on 23-April-2026

                                        273

                                        The National Anthem of Pakistan is written by?

                                        Correct Answer: Hafiz Jalandhri
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Writer: Hafeez Jullundhri  in 1952
                                        • Composer: Ahmed G. Chagla in 1949.
                                        • Duration: Approximately 80 seconds
                                        • Structure: Three stanzas with no repeated verses
                                        • First Recorded: 1954 by Ahmed Rushdi, after 68
                                        • Officially adopted on 13 August 1954.
                                        • 15 lines 
                                        • 48 Words 
                                        • Played for the first time in front of Raza Shah Rahalvi of Iran
                                        • " Jan-i-Istaqbal" stands for the Symbol of ambitions.





                                        اس کے متعلق سوالات پڑھیں

                                        National Symbols

                                        Last updated by NA on 23-April-2026








                                        274

                                        Who is considered the first poet of Sindhi language?

                                        Correct Answer: Qazi Qadan
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Qazi Qadan is the first Sindhi Sufi poet of the Sindi
                                        • Born: 1493 CE, Sehwan (Sindh, Pakistan)
                                        • Died: 1551 CE, Medina, Arabia
                                        • Era: Samma dynasty (Sindh)
                                        • Also known as: Qazi Abu Saeed Zain ud-Din Bukhari



                                        Last updated on NA 23-April-2026

                                        275

                                        Who became the youngest Pakistani bowler to take a PSL hat-trick?

                                        Correct Answer: Ali Raza
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation

                                        Peshawar Zalmi fast bowler Ali Raza became the youngest Pakistani bowler to take a hat-trick in Pakistan Super League (PSL) history on April 22, 2026.

                                        He achieved this feat against the Karachi Kings in PSL 11, with 4-41 in his four-over spell.

                                        He achieved this milestone at the age of 18.

                                        ***

                                         

                                        276

                                        The Siachen conflict was started in which year?

                                        Correct Answer: 1984
                                        Show explanation
                                        Explanation
                                        • Start Year: 1984
                                        • End Year: 2003
                                        • Operation: Operation Meghdoot
                                        • Location: Siachen Glacier (Karakoram Range)
                                        • Countries involved: Pakistan & India
                                        • Known as highest battlefield in the world

                                          Last verified on 23-04-2026

                                          277

                                          In which reforms Muslims demand of separate electorate was incorporated/introduced?

                                          Correct Answer: Minto-Morley Reforms
                                          Show explanation
                                          Explanation
                                          • The Indian Councils Act 1909, also called the Morley-Minto Reforms, introduced separate electorates for Muslims.

                                          Additional information:

                                          • Date of introduction: 12 March 1909
                                          • Total members in Central Legislative Council: 60
                                          • Muslim reserved seats: 5
                                          • 27 members were to be elected from different constituencies.
                                          • Nehru Report (28 August 1928) rejected the demand for separate electorate.

                                          Last verified on 07-03-2026

                                          278

                                          Quit India Movement was launched by the Congress after the failure of ____?

                                          Correct Answer: Cripps Mission
                                          Show explanation
                                          Explanation
                                          • The Quit India Movement was launched in August 1942 by the Indian National Congress.
                                            • It came after the failure of the Cripps Mission.
                                            • The mission failed to satisfy Indian demands for independence.
                                            • As a result, a mass movement was started against British rule.

                                            Additional information:

                                            • Leader: Mahatma Gandhi
                                            • Slogan: “Do or Die”

                                            Last verified on 23-04-2026

                                            279

                                            The Khyber Pass links Pakistan with which Afghan city?

                                            Correct Answer: kabul
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • The Khyber Pass connects Peshawar in Pakistan with Kabul
                                            • Traversed by: N-5 National Highway; Khyber Pass Railway
                                            • Connects Landi Kotal to the Valley of Peshawar.
                                            • Range: White Mountains (Spin Ghar, Safed Koh)
                                            • Location: in Landi Kotal and Jamrud
                                            • Elevation: 1,070 m (3,510 ft)

                                            Last updated question by NA 22-April-2026

                                            280

                                            How many countries share border with Pakistan?

                                            Correct Answer: 4
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            Pakistan shares land borders with four neighboring countries
                                            1. India (East): 2,100 km (East)
                                            2. Afghanistan (Northwest): 2,611 km (West/Northwest)
                                            3. Iran (Southwest): 909 km (West)
                                            4. China (Northeast): 599 km (Northeast)
                                            Other borders:
                                            • Arabian Sea coast: 1,058 km (South)
                                            • Total land border: 6,219 km
                                            • Land + Sea border: 7,277 km
                                            • Land + Sea + LOC & Working Boundary: 8,340 km
                                            Last updated question by NA 22-April-2026
                                            281

                                            Dhullian oil field is located in which area?

                                            Correct Answer: Khaur (Attock)
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • The Dhulian oil field is located in the central Potwar Plateau
                                            • In the Attock District of Punjab
                                            • Situated near the Khaur area
                                            • 90 km southwest of Islamabad
                                            • Discovered in 1937
                                            • Operated by Pakistan Oilfields Limited (POL)


                                            Last updated by NA on 22-April-2026

                                            282

                                            Palermo port is located in which country?

                                            Correct Answer: Italy
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • The Port of Palermo is located in Italy.
                                            • Location: Northern Sicily, Italy
                                            • Sea: Tyrrhenian Sea
                                            • Major Mediterranean cruise and ferry terminal



                                            Last updated by NA on 20-April-2026

                                            283

                                            Imran Khan was sworn in as Pakistan's _____ Prime Minister.

                                            Correct Answer: 22nd
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • Imran Khan was sworn in as Pakistan's 22nd Prime Minister on August 18, 2018.
                                            • He assumed office after his party, PTI, won the general elections in 2018.
                                            • He remained PM for: 43 months 23 days.
                                            • He became PM: August 2018
                                            • He removed: 10 April 2022

                                            Last verified on 22-04-2026

                                            284

                                            Nuskha hai wafa is the poetic collection of?

                                            Correct Answer: Faiz Ahmed Faiz
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • Nuskha Hai Wafa is a poetic collection by Faiz Ahmed Faiz.
                                            • Hr is a famous progressive Urdu poet.
                                            • He is known for revolutionary, romantic, and socially conscious poetry.

                                            Last verified on 22-04-2026

                                            285

                                            The Federal Budget for FY 2019-2020 was passed by the National Assembly on _____?

                                            Correct Answer: June 28
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • The Finance Bill 2019–20 was approved on 28 June 2019 (Friday evening).
                                            • It carried a total budget outlay of Rs 7.022 trillion.
                                            • The approval was completed by the National Assembly of Pakistan.
                                            • The bill was passed despite opposition resistance.
                                            • It is part of Pakistan’s constitutional financial process (Article 77).

                                            Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                            286

                                            Who is the current Secretary Pakistan Hockey Federation?

                                            Correct Answer: Akmal Aziz
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • Brigadier (Retd) Akmal Aziz serves as the Secretary General of the Pakistan Hockey Federation (PHF).
                                            • He was appointed in February 2026

                                            Last verified on 26-05-2026

                                            287

                                            Syed Ameer Ali established Muslim League in London in ______?

                                            Correct Answer: 1908
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • Sayyid Ameer Ali founded the London Muslim Federation in 1908.
                                            • This organization was an independent organization and was not a branch of the All-India Muslim Federation.
                                            • In 1909 he became the first Indian to sit as a member of the Privy Council's judicial committee.
                                            • He served that role until his death in 1928.

                                            Last verified on 04-04-2026

                                            288

                                            Under the 26th Amendment, the seats of tribal districts in NA will be retained at _____?

                                            Correct Answer: 12
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • Amendment: 26th Constitutional Amendment (Pakistan)
                                            • Area: Former FATA (now Tribal Districts of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa)
                                            • Seats in NA: 12 reserved seats retained
                                            • Purpose: Ensure political representation of tribal population

                                            Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                            289

                                            In which area of Pakistan 2000 Old Forest (Oldest Forest of Pakistan) is found?

                                            Correct Answer: Ziarat
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • The oldest forest in Pakistan is found in Ziarat (Juniper Forest).
                                            • It is famous for ancient juniper trees, some over 2000 years old.
                                            • Ziarat Juniper Forest is sometimes called a “living museum of ancient trees” due to its extreme age and ecological importance.
                                            • Changa Manga (largest planted forest in Pakistan)

                                            Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                            290

                                            On May 02, Pakistan's largest solar wall a capacity of generating 410 kilowatt was inaugurated at _____?

                                            Correct Answer: UET Lahore
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • Pakistan inaugurated a solar energy project (solar wall) on May 02, 2019 at UET Lahore.
                                            • It has a capacity of generating 410 kilowatts of electricity.
                                            • The project promotes renewable and clean energy in universities.
                                            • It was installed at a major engineering university in Punjab.

                                            Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                            291

                                            In which of the following cities All India Muslim League was found?

                                            Correct Answer: Dhaka
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • Founded on: 30 December 1906
                                            • Place: Dhaka (then Bengal, British India)
                                            • Founders: Nawab Salimullah, Agha Khan III, etc.
                                            • Objective: Protection of Muslim political rights
                                            • Role: Key political force behind Pakistan Movement

                                            Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                            292

                                            Khunjerab Pass is situated in which range?

                                            Correct Answer: Karakoram
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • Khunjerab Pass is a high mountain pass in Pakistan.
                                            • It is located in Gilgit–Baltistan (Hunza & Nagar region).
                                            • It connects Pakistan with China (border point).
                                            • It lies in the Karakoram Mountain Range.
                                            • It is also known as the Friendship Gate.

                                            Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                            293

                                            Who was the shortest-serving Chief Justice of Pakistan?

                                            Correct Answer: Muhammad Shahabuddin
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • The Chief Justice of Pakistan (CJP) is the head of the Supreme Court.
                                              • Muhammad Shahabuddin served the shortest tenure.
                                              • He was the 3rd Chief Justice of Pakistan.
                                              • His tenure lasted only about 10 days (3 May – 12 May 1960).

                                              Additional information:

                                              • The longest-serving Chief Justice of Pakistan was Mohammad Haleem.
                                              • He served for nearly 9 years (1981–1989).

                                              Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                              294

                                              In _____ amendment of Constitution of Pakistan, NWFP became Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

                                              Correct Answer: 18th
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • NWFP renamed: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
                                              • Date: 19 April 2010
                                              • Constitutional change: 18th Amendment
                                              • Votes: 90 in favor
                                              • Old province: Established in 1901
                                              • 1947 situation: Indian National Congress was influential in NWFP

                                              Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                              295

                                              Through which constitutional amendment, Federally Administered Tribal Areas (FATA) were merged with Khyber Pakhtunkhwa?

                                              Correct Answer: 25th
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              An Act further to amend the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan. Passed, In National Assembly: May 24th, 2018

                                              296

                                              Pakistan took first structural adjustment package from IMF during the regime of ______ in year 1958.

                                              Correct Answer: Ayub Khan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • First IMF Loan: December 8, 1958.
                                              • Government: General Ayub Khan's era.
                                              • Loan Amount: US$25,000 (Equivalent to $272,463 in 2026).
                                              • Purpose: Economic stabilization and fiscal management

                                              Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                              297

                                              In April 2026, Pakistan Navy successfully tested which cruise missile in live firing drill?

                                              Correct Answer: Taimoor
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              On April 21, 2026, Pakistan Navy conducted successful live weapon firing of Taimoor Air-Launched Cruise Missile – an indigenously developed Anti-Ship Weapon System – in a powerful demonstration of precision strike capability and operational readiness.
