                                              298

                                              On _____, former Foreign Minister of Pakistan Abdul Sattar passed away.

                                              Correct Answer: June 23
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Full name: Abdul Sattar
                                              • Death date: 23 June 2019
                                              • Age at death: 88 years
                                              • Position: Foreign Minister (1993, 1999–2002)
                                              • Key event: Agra Summit (2001)

                                              Note: He is distinct from the famous philanthropist Abdul Sattar Edhi, who passed away on July 8, 2016.

                                              Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                              299

                                              The archaeological site Takht-i-bahi is situated in _____?

                                              Correct Answer: Mardan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Location: Mardan, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan
                                              • Era: 1st century CE (Buddhist period)
                                              • Civilization: Gandhara Civilization
                                              • UNESCO status: World Heritage Site (1980)
                                              • Structure: Monastery, stupas, prayer halls, and meditation cells

                                              Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                              300

                                              When Tethyan Copper Company registered case against Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: 12th January 2012
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The Tethyan Copper Company (TCC) filed an international arbitration case against Pakistan on 12th January 2012.
                                              • The dispute was related to the Reko Diq gold and copper mining project (Balochistan).
                                              • The case was registered at the International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes (ICSID).
                                              • It became one of Pakistan’s most important mining-related legal cases.

                                              Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                              301

                                              In 1912, _____ issued Urdu Newspaper Al-Hilal.

                                              Correct Answer: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Newspaper name: Al-Hilal
                                              • Founded: 1912
                                              • Language: Urdu
                                              • Founder: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad
                                              • Purpose: To awaken political and Islamic consciousness among Muslims

                                              Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                              302

                                              Pakistan and India join Shanghai cooperation organization (SCO) in ______?

                                              Correct Answer: 2017, June 9th
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • On 9 June, 2017 India and Pakistan became full members of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO).
                                              • The SCO Headquarters: Beijing, China
                                              • The SCO Founded: June 15, 2001
                                              • The SCO currently comprises of nine Members States (China, India, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Russia, Pakistan, Tajikistan, Iran and Uzbekistan), four Observer States

                                              Last verified on 09-03-2026

                                              303

                                              National Assembly adopted the 26th Constitutional (Amendment) Bill, 2019 on _____?

                                              Correct Answer: 13th May
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The National Assembly of Pakistan adopted the 26th Constitutional (Amendment) Bill, 2019 on 13th May.
                                              • This bill is part of Pakistan’s constitutional reform process.
                                              • It was passed to make changes in the legal and judicial framework.

                                              Last verified on 21-04-2026

                                              304

                                              The 26th amendment was passed by national assembly of Pakistan on 13 May in which year?

                                              Correct Answer: 2024
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Twenty-Sixth Amendment to the Constitution of Pakistan was passed by the parliament between 20 and 21 October 2024.


                                              Passed by the National Assembly of Pakistan


                                              Assented to by: President Asif Zardari

                                              305

                                              In April 2026, Pakistan achieved a major export breakthrough with Russia in which sector?

                                              Correct Answer: Fisheries/Seafood
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistan achieved a major export breakthrough with Russia in the fisheries/seafood sector in April 2026.

                                              Pakistan has secured access to Russia’s $3 billion seafood import market, after approval was granted to 16 local seafood processing plants to export their products, marking a major breakthrough for the country’s fisheries sector.

                                              ***

                                              306

                                              Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad means ______?

                                              Correct Answer: Elimination of discord
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad means “Elimination of Strife (جھگڑے کا خاتمہ)”.
                                              • It was launched on 22 February 2017 in Pakistan.
                                              • The operation was initiated under Qamar Javed Bajwa.
                                              • Its main aim was to eliminate terrorism and ensure lasting peace.
                                              • It is a nationwide security operation.

                                              Last verified on 20-04-2026

                                              307

                                              Pervez Musharraf became 10th president of Pakistan in the year_?

                                              Correct Answer: 2001
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Pervez Musharraf became the 10th President
                                              • He was sworn in on June 20, 2001
                                              • He served as president from 2001 to 2008


                                              Additional information

                                              Know everything about Musharraf

                                              Born: Delhi (August 11, 1943)

                                              Graduated: Pakistan Military Academy at Kakul (1964)

                                              Appointed chief of army staff: October 1998

                                              Appointer: Nawaz Sharif

                                              Coup: October 12, 1999 (declared a state of emergency)

                                              In 2006, titled In the Line of Fire was published.

                                              In March 2007, the then-chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry was suspended.

                                              State of emergency on November 3, 2007.

                                              Resigned from: the army chief in November 2007

                                              Replacer: General Ashfaq Pervaiz Kayani

                                              Musharraf lifted the emergency on December 15, 2007.

                                              In 2010, Musharraf launched his political party — All Pakistan Muslim League (APML).

                                              He flew to Dubai on March 17, 2016

                                              On December 17, 2019, a special court handed Musharraf death sentence in the high treason case (only military ruler to be sentenced to death)

                                              Died: 5 February 2023

                                              Dying place: Dubai, UAE

                                              Disease: amyloidosis

                                              Aged: 79

                                              Buried: Karachi

                                               Last updated by NS 19 April 2026

                                               

                                              308

                                              The "COVID-19 pandemic" reached Pakistan in which year?

                                              Correct Answer: 2020
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The COVID-19 first appeared in late 2019.
                                              • It reached Pakistan in February 2020.
                                              • The first confirmed cases were reported in Karachi.
                                              • The virus spread globally and was declared a pandemic by WHO in March 2020.


                                              Last updated by NS 19 April 2026

                                              309

                                              Where is located highest ATM point of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: Khunjerab Pass
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Location: Khunjerab Pass, Karakoram Range, Pakistan-China border.
                                              • Altitude: 4,693 meters (approx. 15,397 feet).
                                              • Installed by: National Bank of Pakistan (NBP) in 2016


                                              Last updated by NS 19 April 2026

                                              310

                                              Who becomes Pakistan’s youngest aerospace engineer?

                                              Correct Answer: Arooba Faridi
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Arooba Faridi started her journey in aviation by working on piston-engine aircraft (Aeroplane Piston category).
                                              • She later progressed into professional licensing in aviation.
                                              • She registered for EASA Part-66 exams.
                                              • EASA Part-66 is issued under the European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) framework.

                                              Last verified on 19-04-2026

                                              311

                                              The reconcile of Hindus and Muslims a new religion Din-i-Illahi was introduced by?

                                              Correct Answer: Akbar
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Akbar introduced Din-i-Ilahi in 1582.

                                              • It was a syncretic belief system aimed at religious harmony.
                                              • The idea was to promote unity between Hindus and Muslims in the Mughal Empire.
                                              • It was also called Tawhid-i-Ilahi (Divine Monotheism) in historical records.
                                              • Din-i-Ilahi was centered in Fatehpur Sikri.
                                              • It was recorded by court historian Abu’l-Fazl.

                                              Last verified on 19-04-2026

                                              312

                                              Lahore Fort was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in?

                                              Correct Answer: 1981
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Lahore Fort was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1981.
                                              • It is located in Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan.
                                              • It was built by Mughal Emperor Akbar.
                                              • It is also known as Shahi Qila.

                                              Last verified on 19-04-2026

                                              313

                                              The Kuka movement started in mid-Nineteenth century in ______?

                                              Correct Answer: Western Punjab
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The Kuka Movement was a religious and reformist movement in the mid-19th century.
                                              • It started in Western Punjab under the leadership of Bhagat Jawahar Mal (Ram Singh) in 1862.
                                              • The aim was to bring social and religious reforms among Sikhs and society.
                                              • It opposed British rule and corruption.
                                              • The movement played a role in early anti-colonial resistance in India.

                                              Last verified on 19-04-2026

                                              314

                                              Iqbal Day is celebrated on _____?

                                              Correct Answer: 9th November
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Allama Iqbal

                                              • Born: November 9, 1877 (Sialkot)
                                              • Father: Sheikh Noor Muhammad
                                              • Mother: Imam Bibi
                                              • Received PhD: Germany
                                              • Title: Poet of the East
                                              • Famous books: Bang-e-Dara, Zarb-i-Kalim, Armughan-e-Hijaz
                                              • Famous Punjabi Books/Poem: Piyaara Jedi, Baba Bakri Wala
                                              • Died: 21 April 1938
                                              • Burial: Badshahi Mosque in Lahore

                                              Last verified on 30-03-2026

                                              315

                                              Which provincial unanimously adopted resolution recommending Nobel Peace Prize for PM Shahbaz and CDF Asim Munir in 2026?

                                              Correct Answer: Punjab
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Punjab assembly unanimously adopted resolution recommending Nobel Peace Prize for Prime Minister Shahbaz Sharif and Chairman of Defence Forces Field Marshal Asim Munir on 16 April,  2026.

                                              Nominated for their efforts for peace amid the war in the Middle East.

                                              ***

                                              316

                                              Which Mughal emperor abolished the Jizya tax?

                                              Correct Answer: Akbar
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The Mughal emperor Akbar abolished the jizya tax on non-Muslim subjects in 1564
                                              •  Akbar, the third Mughal emperor
                                              317

                                              The Bajaur attack in the early 2026 was claimed by which terrorist outfit?

                                              Correct Answer: TTP
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              A suicide vehicle bombing was followed by a small-arms assault on a security checkpoint in Bajaur District in KPK.

                                              The attack killed 11 security personnel and one child, and wounded seven others.

                                              This incident took place on 16 Feb, 2026.

                                               

                                              The Pakistani Taliban (Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan – TTP) claimed responsibility.

                                              ***

                                              318

                                              As per Article 80(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Federal Government shall, in respect of every financial year, cause to be laid before the National Assembly a statement of the estimated receipt and expenditure of the Federal Government for that year, in Part III, referred to as the __________ .

                                              Correct Answer: Annual Budget Statement
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The provision is contained in Article 80(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973.
                                              • It requires the Federal Government to present an annual financial statement before the National Assembly.
                                              • It is officially referred to as the Annual Budget Statement.


                                              Last updated by NS on 17-April-2026

                                              319

                                              As per Article 1(3) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, __________ may by law admit into the Federation new States or areas on such terms and conditions as it thinks fit.

                                              Correct Answer: Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament)
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • It deals with admission of new areas into Pakistan.
                                              • Authority lies with Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament).
                                              • Parliament can admit new states or areas.
                                              • Admission is done through law.
                                              • Terms and conditions are decided by Parliament.


                                              Last updated by NS on 16-April-2026

                                              320

                                              As per Article 194 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, before entering upon office, the Chief Justice of a High Court shall make before the Governor, and any other Judge of the Court shall make before the Chief Justice, oath in the form set out in the ______ Schedule.

                                              Correct Answer: Third
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Article 194 of the Constitution of Pakistan, 1973 deals with the oath of office of High Court judges.
                                              • The Chief Justice of a High Court takes oath before the Governor.
                                              • Other judges take oath before the Chief Justice of the High Court.
                                              • The oath is prescribed in the Third Schedule of the Constitution.

                                              Last updated on NS-16-April-2026

                                              321

                                              As per Article 179 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, a Judge of the ____ shall hold office until age 65 years.

                                              Correct Answer: Supreme Court
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Article 179 of the Constitution of Pakistan, 1973 deals with the retirement age of judges.
                                              • It states that a Judge of the Supreme Court shall hold office until the age of 65 years.
                                              • This applies to all judges, including the Chief Justice of Pakistan.


                                              Last updated on NS-16-April-2026
                                              322

                                              As per Article 73(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, when a ____ is presented in the National Assembly, a copy shall be transmitted to the Senate

                                              Correct Answer: Money Bill
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • As per Article 73(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan, 1973, a Money Bill is presented in the National Assembly.
                                              • After presentation, a copy is transmitted to the Senate for recommendations.
                                              • The Senate cannot reject or amend a Money Bill, only suggest changes.


                                              Last updated on NS-16-April-2026

                                              323

                                              As per the case Khan Asfandyar Wali and others vs. Federation of Pakistan (PLD 2001 S.C. 607), the decision of the case was that the effect of _____ was valid retrospective.

                                              Correct Answer: National Accountability Bureau Ordinance 1999
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                            • The case Khan Asfandyar Wali vs. Federation of Pakistan (PLD 2001 SC 607) examined the validity of NAB laws.

                                            • The National Accountability Bureau Ordinance, 1999 (NAB Ordinance) was challenged on multiple grounds.
                                            • The 17-judge bench upheld the Ordinance 

                                            • Last updated on NS-16-April-2026
                                              • 324

                                              As per Article 11 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, all forms of forced labour and traffic in human beings are ___?

                                              Correct Answer: Prohibited
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                            • Article 11 of the Constitution of Pakistan deals with prohibition of slavery and forced labour.
                                            • It clearly states that all forms of forced labour are prohibited.

                                            • It also declares that traffic in human beings is prohibited.


                                            • Last updated on NS-16-April-2026
                                              • 325

                                              As per case "The State vs Dosso and Other (PLD 1958 S.C 533)", who was the Chief Justice at that time?

                                              Correct Answer: Justice Muhammad Munir
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The case The State vs Dosso was decided in 1958.
                                              • At that time, the Chief Justice of Pakistan was Muhammad Munir.
                                              • He authored the judgment and applied the Doctrine of Necessity.
                                              • The decision validated the martial law imposed by General Ayub Khan.

                                              Last updated on NS-16-April-2026

                                              326

                                              Pakistan test-fired which type of missile in April 2026?

                                              Correct Answer: Anti-ship missile
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistan conducted a successful test of a locally developed anti-ship missile that accurately hit its target at extended range.

                                              The Pakistan Navy has successfully fired a locally developed anti-ship ballistic missile to engage targets at an extended range on April 16, 2026.

                                              ***

                                              327

                                              Who is the Current T20 Captain of Pakistan Cricket Team?

                                              Correct Answer: Salman Ali Agha
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              T20I captain: Salman Ali Agha

                                              Test captain: Shan Masood

                                              One Day captain: Shaheen Shah Afridi

                                              328

                                              Which of the following resolutions is the preamble of the 1973's constitution of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: Objective Resolution (1949)
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The preamble of the Constitution of Pakistan is based on the Objectives Resolution.
                                              • It was passed on 12 March 1949 by the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan.
                                              • It was presented by Prime Minister Liaquat Ali Khan.
                                              • It became part of the Constitution through Article 2A (1985).

                                              Last verified on 13-04-2026

                                              329

                                              The Radcliff Award is known for?

                                              Correct Answer: Boundary Commission
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The Radcliff Award is known for Boundary Commission.

                                              Additional information:

                                              Key facts about Radcliffe Award:

                                              • Partition Plan Date: 3 June 1947
                                              • Announcement Date: 17 August 1947
                                              • Chairman: Cyril Radcliffe
                                              • Provinces Divided: Punjab & Bengal
                                              • Countries Involved: India & Pakistan

                                              Last verified on 13-04-2026

                                              330

                                              The historic Summit of the Organization of Islamic Countries held in Lahore was its?

                                              Correct Answer: Second Summit
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Pakistan hosted the 2nd Islamic Summit Conference of the OIC from 22–24 February 1974 in Lahore.
                                              • An Extraordinary OIC Summit was also held later in 1997 in Islamabad on Pakistan’s Golden Jubilee.

                                              Additional information:

                                              • 1st Summit: Rabat, Morocco – September 1969
                                              • 3rd Summit: Makkah, Saudi Arabia – 25–28 January 1981

                                              Last verified on 07-03-2026

                                              331

                                              Pakistan is situated in the following imaginary equator line?

                                              Correct Answer: Tropic of Cancer
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Pakistan lies near the Tropic of Cancer.
                                              • This line passes through the southern part of Pakistan.

                                              Additional information:

                                              Important imaginary latitude lines:

                                              • Equator – 0°
                                              • Tropic of Cancer – 23.5° North
                                              • Tropic of Capricorn – 23.5° South
                                              • Arctic Circle – 66.5° North
                                              • Antarctic Circle – 66.5° South

                                              Last verified on 13-04-2026

                                              332

                                              The Khewra Salt Mines are located in _____?

                                              Correct Answer: Jehlum
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Khewra Salt Mine is the largest and oldest salt mine in Pakistan and the second-largest in the world.
                                              • It is located in Khewra near Pind Dadan Khan, Jhelum District, Punjab.

                                              Additional information:

                                              • It is also called the Mayo Salt Mine.
                                              • The salt deposits were discovered during the campaign of Alexander the Great in 326 BC.
                                              • The mine was officially opened in 1872 during the British period.

                                              Last verified on 14-03-2026

                                              333

                                              Which countries was hosted ICC Champions Trophy 2025?

                                              Correct Answer: Pakistan & UAE
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Primary Host: Pakistan (PCB)
                                              • Neutral Venue: Dubai, United Arab Emirates (UAE)
                                              • Edition: 9th
                                              • Participating teams: 8
                                              • Total matches: 15
                                              • Tournament dates: 19 Feb 2025 – 09 March 2025.
                                              • Winner: India

                                               Last updated NA 12-04-2026

                                              334

                                              Liaqat Ali Khan Assassinated in which city?

                                              Correct Answer: Rawalpindi
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Liaquat Ali Khan was assassinated in Rawalpindi 
                                              • On 16 October 1951 during a public meeting.
                                              • Party: Muslim League
                                              • Pakistani statesman, lawyer, and political theorist.
                                              • known as Quaid-e-Millat or Shaheed-e-Millat,
                                              • Leading founding fathers of Pakistan. 
                                              • Born: October 1, 1895, Karnal, India


                                               Last updated 12 april 2026

                                              335

                                              The Muhammad Ali Bogra Formula was presented as a framework for?

                                              Correct Answer: A federal constitution
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The Muhammad Ali Bogra Formula (1953) was a constitutional proposal.
                                              • It aimed to resolve political imbalance in Pakistan.
                                              • It focused on fair representation of East and West Pakistan.
                                              • It was designed to reduce constitutional deadlock.
                                              • It became part of efforts to frame a federal constitution.

                                              Last verified on 11-04-2026

                                              336

                                              The Water Apportionment Accord of 1991 deals with the distribution of water resources among?

                                              Correct Answer: The provinces of Pakistan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                            • The Water Apportionment Accord (WAA) was signed by provinces on 16 March 1991.
                                            • It was signed to distribute river water among provinces.
                                            • It was approved by the Council of Common Interests (CCI) on 21 March 1991.
                                            • Clause 13 of WAA recognized the need to establish the Indus River System Authority (IRSA).
                                            • IRSA was created to implement the Accord.
                                            • Last verified on 11-04-2026
                                              • 337

                                              Comparing the economic policies of the Ayub Khan era (1958-1969) and the Zulifqar Ali Bhutto era (1971-1977) reveals a fundamental shift from?

                                              Correct Answer: Industrial growth via the private sector to a policy of nationalization and socialist reforms
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Ayub Khan Era (1958–69) – Strong industrialization through private investment
                                              • Known as “Decade of Development” due to rapid economic growth
                                              • Bhutto Era (1971–77) – Nationalization of industries, banks, and schools
                                              • Major reforms in labor laws and land reforms under Bhutto
                                              • Shift from capitalist economy → socialist economic model

                                              Last verified on 10-04-2026

                                              338

                                              The primary consequence of the Liaquat-Nehru Pact (1950) was that it?

                                              Correct Answer: Mechanism to protect the rights of minorities in both countries
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                            • The Liaquat-Nehru Pact, also called the Delhi Pact.
                                            • It was signed on April 8, 1950.
                                            • It was an agreement between India and Pakistan to address minority rights.
                                            • The pact was signed by PM Jawaharlal Nehru of India and PM Liaquat Ali Khan of Pakistan.
                                            • Last verified on 10-04-2026
                                              • 339

                                              Name the institution in Pakistan responsible for formulating the country's five-year plans for economic development?

                                              Correct Answer: Planning Commission
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Pakistan’s Five-Year Plans are prepared to guide national economic development.
                                              • The main institution responsible is the Planning Commission of Pakistan.
                                              • It sets development goals for industries, agriculture, and infrastructure.
                                              • First Five-Year Plan – 1955–1960

                                              Last verified on 10-04-2026

                                              340

                                              A country produces a large surplus of cotton cloth and sells it to other nations. This activity is an example of?

                                              Correct Answer: Export
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • When a country sells its surplus goods to other countries (foreign), it is called export.
                                              • Cotton cloth is a common export product in many countries.

                                              Additional information:

                                              • Import – Buying goods from foreign countries into home country
                                              • Domestic trade – Trade within the same country
                                              • Pakistan’s major exports: textiles, rice, sports goods, leather

                                              Last verified on 10-04-2026

                                              341

                                              Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto introduced the policy of nationalization in?

                                              Correct Answer: 1972
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • 1972 – First phase: 31 major industries nationalized (textile, cement, etc.)
                                              • 1974 – Banks and insurance companies were nationalized
                                              • 1972 – Educational institutions also came under government control
                                              • Steel Mills (Pakistan Steel Mills) – Established during Bhutto era (1973)
                                              • Agricultural reforms (1972–1977) – Land ceiling policies introduced

                                              Last verified on 10-04-2026

                                              342

                                              In April 2026, which province passed resolution calling for PM Shehbaz Sharif & Field Marshal Asim Munir to nominate for Nobel Peace Prize?

                                              Correct Answer: Punjab
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              In the early April 2026, Punjab province in Pakistan passed the resolution calling for the nomination of Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, Chief of Defence Forces/COAS Field Marshal Asim Munir & Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar for the Nobel Peace Prize, citing their diplomatic role in easing US-Iran war.

                                              ***

                                              343

                                              The following is a major Kharif crop in Pakistan, sown at the beginning of the monsoon season?

                                              Correct Answer: Rice
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Major Kharif Crops

                                              1. Rice
                                              2. Sugarcane
                                              3. Cotton
                                              4. Maize
                                              5. Bajra
                                              6. Jowar
                                              Additional information:
                                              Kharif Crop Period
                                              • Start: April – June
                                              • End: September – October
                                              Rabi Crop Period
                                              • Sowing: October – December
                                              • Harvesting: March – May

                                              Last verified on 25-01-2026

                                              344

                                              Who is the present Governor of Gilgit Baltistan?

                                              Correct Answer: Syed Mehdi Shah
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Syed Mehdi Shah Shah became the 8th Governor of Gilgit-Baltistan in 2022.

                                              Previously, Raja Jalal Hussain Maqpoon was governor Gilgit-Baltistan.

                                              Last Verified on 09-04-2026

                                              345

                                              Who has become youngest dual PhD holder in Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: Dr. Sumaira
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Dr. Sumaira Khowaja-Punjwani has become Pakistan’s youngest dual PhD holder.

                                              She has become the first registered nurse in the country to earn dual PhD status.

                                              As of April 2026, she is the principal of the College of Nursing & Midwifery at Ziauddin University.

                                              ***

                                              346

                                              Basic Democracies System was for the first time implemented in Pakistan in the year _____?

                                              Correct Answer: 1959
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The Basic Democracies System was implemented on 26 October 1959
                                              • After the imposition of Martial Law in 1958,
                                              • General Muhammad Ayub Khan introduced 80,000 Basic Democrats


                                              347

                                              Who became third prime minister in the history of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: Mohammad Ali Bogra
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The third prime minister of Pakistan was Mohammad Ali Bogra
                                              • Term: April 17, 1953 – August 11, 1955.
                                              • Party: Muslim League.
                                              • Predecessor: Khawaja Nazimuddin.
                                              • Successor: Chaudhri Muhammad Ali.

                                              ****


                                              List of Prime Ministers of Pakistan 

                                               Term#   Name

                                              1              Liaquat Ali Khan

                                              2              Sir Khawaja Nazimuddin

                                              4              Chaudhry Mohammad Ali

                                              9              Zulfikar Ali Bhutto

                                              11           Benazir Bhutto

                                              12           Nawaz Sharif

                                              13           Benazir Bhutto

                                              14           Nawaz Sharif

                                              16           Chaudhry Shujaat Hussain

                                              17           Shaukat Aziz

                                              18           Yousaf Raza Gillani

                                              348

                                              The main issue of Northern Areas is?

                                              Correct Answer: Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs)
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              In the Northern Areas of Pakistan, Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) are a major hazard

                                              349

                                              The Medieval Period started in?

                                              Correct Answer: 476 AD
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Note: In European history, the Medieval period typically refers to a different timeframe, usually 476 AD to 1450/1500 A


                                              • The medieval period in India
                                              • Have started with the arrival of Islam and the conquest of Sindh by Muhammad bin Qasim 
                                              • Start Date: 712 AD (Conquest of Sindh).
                                              • End Date: 1857 AD (Fall of the Mughal Empire/start of British Raj).
                                              350

                                              Due to the 2022 floods, the estimated loss to the economy of Pakistan was?

                                              Correct Answer: About $15 billion
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The damage of the flooding was ₨3.91 trillion (US$14.9 billion)
                                              • The economic loss was ₨3.99 trillion (US$15.2 billion).
                                              • World Bank. led a $1.5 billion aid package for Pakistan’s flood relief efforts
                                              351

                                              The Brohi tribe primarily lives in the north of?

                                              Correct Answer: Kalat
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The Brohi tribe primarily lives in the north of Kalat,
                                              • Located within the Sarawan region of Balochistan 
                                              • It is an ethnolinguistic group of pastoralists
                                              352

                                              National Flag of Pakistan was designed/made by _____?

                                              Correct Answer: Amir Uddin Kidwai
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Designer: Amir Uddin Kidwai Adoption Date: 11 August 1947 (officially adopted) Additional information: Green field – represents Muslims of Pakistan White stripe – represents minorities and religious minorities’ rights Crescent and star – symbol of progress and light Proportion of flag: 2:3 (height:width)

                                              Last verified on 16-01-2026

                                              353

                                              Which is the most vulnerable province of Pakistan to climate change?

                                              Correct Answer: Balochistan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Balochistan is widely considered one of the most climate-vulnerable regions in Pakistan

                                              Baluchistan is the biggest province of Pakistan by area.

                                              Became a province in 1970

                                              The capital of Baluchistan is Quetta.

                                              Covers 42 percent of the area of Pakistan

                                              354

                                              Who was the first Muslim ruler of Indian Sub-Continent:

                                              Correct Answer: Qutab-ud-din Aibak
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Qutb ud-Din Aibak was the first independent Muslim ruler of Delhi 
                                              • Founder of the Mamluk (Slave) Dynasty
                                              • He was the general of Muhammad Ghori.

                                              Additional information

                                              • Al-khafif was the first ruler of the Soomra dynasty (1011-1026)
                                              • Mughal emperor Akbar was the first Muslim 
                                              • Razia Sultana, the first woman ruler of India
                                              355

                                              Who was the last Chief (Chairman) of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: General Sahir Shamshad Mirza
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • General Sahir Shamshad Mirza is identified as the last CJCSC of Pakistan
                                              • Role: He was the 18th Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee.
                                              • Tenure: 27 November 2022 – 27 November 2025
                                              356

                                              Who was the first Muslim C-in-C of Air Force?

                                              Correct Answer: Air marshal Asgher Khan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Air Marshal Mohammad Asghar Khan first native Commander-in-Chief of the Pakistan Air Force
                                              • Serving from 1957 to 1965.
                                              • Known as the "Father of the Pakistan Air Force,"
                                              •  Born in Jammu & Kashmir in 1921
                                              •  He passed away on January 5, 2018



                                              ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

                                              Frank Meseri was the first C-in-C of the Pak Army

                                              Paery Cane was the first C-in-C of the Pakistan Air Force

                                              Gefford James was the first C-in-C of the Pakistan Navy

                                              ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

                                              General Takka Khan was the first Chief of Staff of the Pak Army

                                              Zafar Ahmed Chaudhry was the first Chief of Air Staff of the Air Force

                                              Hasan Hafeez Ahmed was the first Chief of Naval Staff

                                              ***

                                              ND2-12-2022

                                              357

                                              Pakistan became Islamic Republic in which constitution _____?

                                              Correct Answer: 1956
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Pakistan became an "Islamic Republic" in its 1956 constitution.
                                              • This constitution was the first to declare Pakistan as an Islamic Republic.
                                              • It is reflecting the country's identity as a democratic state founded on the principles of Islam.

                                              Additional information:

                                              • The constitution was adopted on March 23, 1956.
                                              • The 1956 Constitution was abrogated in 1958.
                                              • When President Iskander Mirza imposed martial law and declared himself the Chief Martial Law Administrator.

                                              Last verified on 23-03-2026

                                              358

                                              Quaid-e-Azam presented his Delhi proposal in ____?

                                              Correct Answer: 1927
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Quaid-e-Azam presented the Delhi proposals in 1927
                                              • The conference was held on March 20, 1927
                                              • Resulting in proposals between Delhi and Muslims

                                              359

                                              The first Mughal Emperor Babar's Tomb situated in _____?

                                              Correct Answer: Kabul
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The tomb of Babur, the first Mughal Emperor, is located in Bagh-e Babur, Kabul (Afghanistan).
                                              • He died on 5 January 1531 in Agra at the age of 47.
                                              • Initially, he was buried in Agra.
                                              • Later, his remains were shifted to Kabul as per his wish.
                                              • He was reburied in Kabul between 1539–1544.

                                              Last verified on 02-04-2026

                                              360

                                              Qutb-ud-Din Aibek died in 1210 while playing _____?

                                              Correct Answer: Polo
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Qutub-ud-din Aibak died in an accident while he was playing polo.
                                              • He fell from a horse and was severely injured.
                                              • He died on 14 November 1210 (aged 60) in Lahore.
                                              • Burial in Anarkali Bazaar, Lahore.
                                              • He was born in 1150 in Turkestan.

                                               Additional information:

                                              • He established the first independent Turkish Kingdom in India.
                                              • He founded the first Islamic state was founded in 1206.
                                              • He became the first Sultan of Delhi from 1206 to 1210.

                                              After Muhammad Ghori's assassination in 1206.

                                              • He established the Delhi Sultanate (1206–1526).
                                              • He started the Mamluk Dynasty or Salve Dynasty.

                                              Last verified on 04-04-2026

                                              361

                                              Thar desert is famous for its _____?

                                              Correct Answer: Sand
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Thar Desert is the largest desert in Pakistan.
                                              • It is famous for its arid climate, sand dunes, and unique culture.
                                              • It is the only subtropical desert in Asia.
                                                • It is the 3rd largest in Asia.
                                                • It spans an area of 175,000 square kilometers.
                                                • It covers large areas of Pakistan and India.

                                                Pakistan hosts five major deserts which were historic forests:

                                                1. Cholistan (Bahawalpur, Punjab)
                                                2. Thal (Bakhar district of Punjab)
                                                3. Katpana desert (Sakardu)
                                                4. Kharan desert (Balochistan)
                                                5. Thar desert (Sindh)

                                                Last updated by NA on 20-April-2026

                                                362

                                                Pakistan's most famous painter is _______?

                                                Correct Answer: Sadeqain
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                                • Syed Sadequain Ahmed is Pakistan’s most famous painter and calligrapher.
                                                • Renowned for his paintings, murals, and Islamic calligraphy.
                                                • Born in Amroha, India, 1930, and later settled in Karachi, Pakistan.
                                                • Died: 10 February 1987, Karachi
                                                • Resting Place: Sakhi Hassan Cemetery, Karachi

                                                Last verified on 04-04-2026

                                                363

                                                Famous love story of “Sohni Mahiwal” is associated with which river?

                                                Correct Answer: Chenab
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                                • Sohni Mahiwal is a famous Punjabi love story.
                                                • It tells the tale of Sohni, a potter’s daughter, and Mahiwal, a buffalo herder.
                                                • Written by Fazal Shah Sayyad.
                                                • Considered one of the four most popular Punjabi romances.
                                                • Their tomb is 75 km from Hyderabad, Pakistan.

                                                Last verified on 03-04-2036

                                                364

                                                Allama Iqbal studied at these universities in Europe?

                                                Correct Answer: Cambridge, Heidelberg
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                Allama Iqbal studied in Europe at:

                                                • University of Cambridge (England)
                                                • Heidelberg University (Germany)
                                                • National poet of Pakistan, pursued higher education in Europe.

                                                 Last verified on 04-04-2026

                                                365

                                                "Mast Tawakali" is a famous poet of _______?

                                                Correct Answer: Balochi language
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                                • Mast Tawakali (1817–1892) was a prominent Sufi poet of Balochistan.
                                                • He was dedicated to the poor and often opposed the oppressive feudal system.
                                                • Contemporary of Khwaja Farid and Mian Muhammad Bakhsh, he also wrote resistance poetry against colonial powers.

                                                Last verified on 03-04-2026

                                                366

                                                Which one of these is first Cruise Missile of Pakistan?

                                                Correct Answer: Hatf VII (Babur)
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                              • Babur (Hatf-VII): Pakistan's first cruise missile, developed in the 1990s.
                                              • First Test: Conducted in August 2005, surprising U.S. intelligence.
                                              • Nuclear Capability: Successfully tested in February 2011.
                                              • Range: Initial range of 350 km; Babur-2/1(B) extends to 700 km.
                                              • Last verified on 03-04-2026
                                                • 367

                                                When did Pakistan become member of IMF?

                                                Correct Answer: 1950
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                                • Pakistan became a member of the IMF and the World Bank in 1950.
                                                • WTO in 1995.

                                                Additional information:

                                                • Founded: 22 July 1944
                                                • Headquarters: Washington, D.C., USA
                                                • Members: 191 countries
                                                • Purpose: Financial stability, economic growth, and balance of payments support
                                                • First Managing Director: Camille Gutt

                                                Last Verified on 13-01-2026

                                                368

                                                Qutb-ud-din Aibak was the slave of _______?

                                                Correct Answer: Muhammad Ghauri
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                                • Qutb Uddin Aibak was a slave and general of Muhammad Ghauri.
                                                • Later becoming the first ruler of the Delhi Sultanate.
                                                • After Ghauri's assassination in 1206, Aibak declared himself Sultan of Delhi and established the Mamluk (Slave) Dynasty.

                                                Last verified on 03-04-2026

                                                369

                                                Which of the following dam is situated at the highest altitude of Pakistan?

                                                Correct Answer: Mohmand Dam
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                Mohmand Dam

                                                • Construction Start: 2 May 2019
                                                • Expected Opening: 2026-27
                                                • Location: Mohmand District, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan

                                                Last verified on 03-04-2026

                                                370

                                                Muhammad Bin Qasim was the relative/nephew of ______?

                                                Correct Answer: Hajjaj bin Yusuf
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                                • Muhammad Bin Qasim was the close relative/nephew of Hajjaj bin Yusuf.
                                                • He was the son-in-law of Hajjaj bin Yousuf.
                                                • Because of him, he was appointed the governor of Persia.
                                                • Hajjaj ibn Yusuf was the governor of the Umayyad Caliphate from 692–694.

                                                  Additional information:

                                                  • Muhammad ibn Qasim led the Muslim conquest of Sindh.
                                                  • From Raja Dahir in the battle of Aror.
                                                  • He was the first Muslim to have successfully captured Hindu territories.
                                                  • He initiated the early Islamic India in 712 AD.

                                                  Last verified on 03-04-2026

                                                  371

                                                  When Decimal System was introduced in Pakistan?

                                                  Correct Answer: 1st January 1961
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • Decimal Coinage in Pakistan: Introduced on 1 January 1961 to simplify currency calculations.
                                                  • Based on the Rupee, divided into 100 paisas.
                                                  • 1961: 1, 5, 10 paisa coins issued
                                                  • 1963: 25 and 50 paisa coins issued
                                                  • First coins ever: 1948

                                                  Last verified on 03-04-2026

                                                  372

                                                  Naseem Hijazi was ______?

                                                  Correct Answer: A historian and novelist
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • Naseem Hijazi (1914–1996) was a renowned Pakistani historian and novelist.
                                                  • He is famous for writing historical novels about Islamic history and Muslim heroes.
                                                  • His works inspire patriotism and understanding of Islamic civilization.
                                                  • Popular novels: Shaheen, Khaak aur Khoon, Dastan-e-Mujahid.

                                                  Last verified on 03-04-2026

                                                  373

                                                  What is the length of Karakoram highway?

                                                  Correct Answer: 810 miles
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • The Karakoram Highway (KKH) is also known as the Friendship Highway in China and N-35 in Pakistan.
                                                  • It was built by the governments of Pakistan and China and completed in 1979.
                                                  • Starting from Hassan Abdal, it spans 1,300 (810 miles) km in Pakistan.
                                                  • The Silk Route is also referred to as the Karakoram Highway.
                                                  • The Karakoram Range, located between China and Pakistan.
                                                  • It is traversed by the Karakoram Highway.
                                                  • It connects the two countries via the Khunjerab Pass.

                                                  Last verified on 03-04-2026

                                                  374

                                                  Five rivers of Pakistan meet at _____?

                                                  Correct Answer: Panjnad River
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • Punjab means “Land of Five Rivers” (Punj = Five, Aab = Water).
                                                  • The five rivers of Punjab are Sutlej, Beas, Ravi, Chenab, and Jhelum.
                                                  • All five rivers merge into the Panjnad River before joining the Indus.
                                                  • Panjnad is the main river system of southern Punjab.

                                                  Last verified on 03-04-2026

                                                  375

                                                  The 1857 War of Independence started from _____?

                                                  Correct Answer: Meerut
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • The Indian Rebellion of 1857, also known as the First War of Independence.
                                                  • It was began on May 10, 1857, at Meerut as a sepoy mutiny.

                                                  Last verified on 03-04-2026

                                                  376

                                                  Pakistan Aeronautical Complex at Kamra was built with the financial and technical assistance of _____?

                                                  Correct Answer: China
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • Pakistan Aeronautical Complex at Kamra is located in Attock, Punjab.
                                                  • It was established with the help of China.
                                                  • Founded: 1971, Kamra Kalan
                                                  • Headquarters: Kamra Kalan
                                                  • Parent organization: Pakistan Air Force
                                                  • Production output: Military Aircraft

                                                  Last verified on 02-04-2026

                                                  377

                                                  Kitab-ul-Hind was written by _____?

                                                  Correct Answer: Al Biruni
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • The author of Kitab-ul-Hind is Al-Biruni.
                                                  • Publisher: Anjuman Taraqqi Urdu (Hind), Delhi.
                                                  • Year of Publication: 1941.
                                                  • The language of Al-Biruni's 'Kitab-ul-Hind' is Arabic.
