                                                  378

                                                  Tarbela Dam is located in which district?

                                                  Correct Answer: Swabi
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • Tarbela Dam was built on the Indus River in KPk.
                                                  • It is located mainly in the Swabi District Tehsil Topi of KPK.
                                                  • It is the largest earth-filled dam in the world
                                                  • The Construction began in 1968  
                                                  • They were Opened in 1976
                                                  • Height: 470 feet (143 m)

                                                  Last verified on 30-03-2026

                                                  379

                                                  Where is Quaid-e-Azam solar park located?

                                                  Correct Answer: Bahalwalpur
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • Quaid-e-Azam Solar Park is located in Bahawalpur.
                                                  • It is in the desert area of Lal Sohanra, Cholistan.
                                                  • It is the largest solar power plant in Pakistan.
                                                  • The park was built in May 2015.
                                                  • Installed capacity is 400–600 MW, generating clean energy.
                                                  • Total cost of construction was $121 million.

                                                  Last verified on 02-04-2026

                                                  380

                                                  The Largest Desert of Pakistan is?

                                                  Correct Answer: Thar Desert
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • Thar Desert is the largest desert in Pakistan.
                                                  • It is the only subtropical desert in Asia.
                                                    • It is the 3rd largest in Asia.
                                                    • It spans an area of 175,000 square kilometers.
                                                    • It covers large areas of Pakistan and India.

                                                    Pakistan hosts five major deserts which were historic forests:

                                                    1. Cholistan (Bahawalpur, Punjab)
                                                    2. Thal (Bakhar district of Punjab)
                                                    3. Katpana desert (Sakardu)
                                                    4. Kharan desert (Balochistan)
                                                    5. Thar desert (Sindh)

                                                    Last verified on 02-04-2026

                                                    381

                                                    Shah Waliullah was born in ______?

                                                    Correct Answer: 1703
                                                    Show explanation
                                                    Explanation
                                                    • Shah Waliullah Dehlawi (1703–1762) was a great Islamic scholar and reformer of the Mughal era.
                                                    • His real name was Qutubuddin Ahmad Syed.
                                                    • He was born on 21 February 1703 in Phulat (India) during the reign of Aurangzeb.
                                                    • He translated the Holy Quran into Persian for the first time in the subcontinent.
                                                    • He wrote about 51 books on Islam, society, and reforms.

                                                    Last verified on 02-04-2026

                                                    382

                                                    On July 9, 1948, Government of Pakistan issued for the very first time?

                                                    Correct Answer: Postal stamp
                                                    Show explanation
                                                    Explanation
                                                    • Pakistan’s first coin was issued on 3rd January 1948.
                                                    • The First Postal Stamp of Pakistan was issued on 9th July 1948.
                                                    • Pakistan’s first currency note was issued on March 1, 1949.

                                                    Last verified on 02-04-2026

                                                    383

                                                    When the Partition of Bengal took place?

                                                    Correct Answer: 1905
                                                    Show explanation
                                                    Explanation
                                                    • The Partition of Bengal, implemented in 1905 by Lord Curzon.
                                                    • It was formally annulled in 1911. 
                                                    • He divided Bengal into two provinces on 16-10-1905.

                                                      The province was divided into:

                                                      • West Bengal, which had a majority of Hindus,
                                                      • East Bengal, which had a majority of Muslims

                                                      Last verified on 30-03-2026

                                                      384

                                                      Rabi season begins in _____?

                                                      Correct Answer: October-December
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation

                                                      Rabi Crop:

                                                      • Start: October-December (winter season)
                                                      • End: March-May (harvesting season)
                                                      • Rabi crops are planted in the winter and harvested in the summer.

                                                      Kharif Crop:

                                                      • Start: June-July (monsoon season)
                                                      • End: September-October (harvesting season)
                                                      • Kharif crops are planted in the monsoon season and harvested in the winter.

                                                      Pakistan's major agricultural seasons:

                                                      Kharif (monsoon) and Rabi (winter).

                                                      Last verified on 02-04-2026

                                                      385

                                                      Pamphlet ‘Now or Never’ is written by Chaudhry Rehmat Ali in which University?

                                                      Correct Answer: Cambridge
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation
                                                      • ‘Now or Never’ is a pamphlet written by Chaudhry Rehmat Ali in 1933.
                                                      • It advocated the creation of a separate Muslim state (Pakistan).
                                                      • The pamphlet was written while he was studying at Cambridge University, England.

                                                      Last verified on 02-04-2026

                                                      386

                                                      Which of the following is the oldest Barrage?

                                                      Correct Answer: Sukkur
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation
                                                      • The Sukkur Barrage, officially named Lloyd Barrage, is located in Sindh Province.
                                                      • It built from 1923 to 1932 during the British Raj.
                                                      • It was rehabilitated in November 2004. 
                                                      • The barrage is approximately 2 km (1 mi) long.
                                                      • It is the oldest in Pakistan, with seven canals taking off from it.

                                                      Last verified on 14-03-2026

                                                      387

                                                      Pakistan standard time is how many hours ahead of GMT?

                                                      Correct Answer: +5 hours
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation
                                                      • Pakistan Standard Time (PST) is 5 hours ahead of Greenwich Mean Time (GMT).
                                                      • Suggested by Professor Muhammad Anwar in 1951.
                                                      • GMT is the standard time of Greenwich, UK, used as a reference worldwide.
                                                      • Example: When it is 12:00 noon GMT, it is 5:00 PM in Pakistan.

                                                      Last verified on 17-03-2026

                                                      388

                                                      Which is the first/old Capital of Pakistan?

                                                      Correct Answer: Karachi
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation
                                                      • Karachi was the first capital of Pakistan, chosen by Muhammad Ali Jinnah in 1947.
                                                      • It remained the capital until 1959.
                                                      • From 1959–1967, Rawalpindi served as the interim capital.
                                                      • Islamabad became the official capital on 14 August 1967.
                                                      • Karachi is in Sindh province.
                                                      • It was the political center at independence.

                                                      Last verified on 02-03-2026


                                                      389

                                                      Which is the highest mountain pass in the world?

                                                      Correct Answer: Khunjerab Pass
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation
                                                      • The Khunjerab Pass is the highest mountain pass in the world.
                                                      • It is located at an elevation of approximately 4,693 meters (15,397 feet).
                                                      • It is situated on the border between Pakistan and China.
                                                      • It connecting the Gilgit-Baltistan region of Pakistan with China's Xinjiang region.

                                                      Last verified on 02-04-2026

                                                      390

                                                      Keenjhar lake is located in which district of _______?

                                                      Correct Answer: Thatta
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation
                                                      • Keenjhar Lake, also known as Kalri Lake.
                                                      • It is one of the largest freshwater lakes in Pakistan.
                                                      • It is located in Thatta District, Sindh, about 122 km from Karachi and 18 km from Thatta city.
                                                      • The lake was formed by merging Sonehri Lake and Keenjhar Lake.
                                                      • It was designated as a Ramsar Wetland Site on 23 July 1976.

                                                      Last verified on 02-04-2026

                                                      391

                                                      When did Khilafat Movement come to an end?

                                                      Correct Answer: 1924
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation
                                                      • The Khilafat Movement came to an end in 1924 when Mustafa Kemal Atatürk abolished the Ottoman Caliphate.
                                                      • The movement started in 1919.
                                                      • It was led by Indian Muslim leaders like Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar and Maulana Shaukat Ali to protect the Caliphate.

                                                      Last verified on 02-04-2026

                                                      392

                                                      The idea of Pakistan was first conceived by _____?

                                                      Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation
                                                      • The idea of Pakistan was first conceived by Muhammad Iqbal.
                                                      • He is widely regarded as the spiritual father of Pakistan.
                                                      • Muhammad Iqbal was famous for his lines "Mazhab Nahi Sikhata Aapas Mein Bair Rakhna".
                                                      • Muhammad Iqbal was the author of Book 'Baang-e-Dara'.

                                                      Last verified on 01-04-2026

                                                      393

                                                      The International Islamic University is in _____?

                                                      Correct Answer: Islamabad
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation
                                                      • Islamic University Islamabad is a major Islamic educational institution in Pakistan.
                                                      • Located in Sector H-10, Islamabad, the campus covers 704 acres.
                                                      • Foundation laid on Muharram 1, 1401 Hijri.
                                                      • Established in 1980 and restructured in 1985.
                                                      • The university focuses on Islamic studies, modern sciences, and research.
                                                      • President: Nauman Bhai
                                                      • Rector: Masoom Yasinzai

                                                      Last verified on 01-04-2026

                                                      394

                                                      The right of separate electorate was given to the Muslims under the Indian Councils Act of _____?

                                                      Correct Answer: 1909
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation
                                                      • The Indian Councils Act 1909, also called the Morley-Minto Reforms, introduced separate electorates for Muslims.

                                                      Additional information:

                                                      • Date of introduction: 12 March 1909
                                                      • Total members in Central Legislative Council: 60
                                                      • Muslim reserved seats: 5
                                                      • 27 members were to be elected from different constituencies.
                                                      • Nehru Report (28 August 1928) rejected the demand for separate electorate.

                                                      Last verified on 01-04-2026

                                                      395

                                                      Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental (MAO) College was founded?

                                                      Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed Khan
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation

                                                      Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College

                                                        • Founder: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan
                                                        • Founded: 1875 (primary), 1877 (college)
                                                        • Foundation stone laid by Lord Lytton on 8 January 1878.
                                                        • Intermediate Classes: 1878
                                                        • B.A. Classes: 1881
                                                        • Upgrade to AMU: 1920

                                                        Last verified on 01-04-2026

                                                        396

                                                        Babusar Pass connects _____?

                                                        Correct Answer: Abbotabad with Gilgit
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation

                                                        Important Passes of Pakistan

                                                        1. Khyber Pass

                                                        • Connects: Peshawar to Kabul
                                                        • Connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan
                                                        • Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 

                                                        2. Bolan Pass 

                                                        • Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 
                                                        • Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 

                                                        3. Lowari Pass

                                                        • Connects: Chitral with Dir 
                                                        • Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 

                                                        4. Broghil Pass

                                                        • Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 
                                                        • Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 

                                                        5. Malakand Pass

                                                        • Connects: Dir with Chitral 
                                                        • Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 

                                                        6. Shandur Pass

                                                        • Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 
                                                        • Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 

                                                        7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan)

                                                        • Other name: Friendship Gate
                                                        • Connects: China with Pakistan 
                                                        • Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 

                                                        8. Dorah Pass

                                                        • Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 
                                                        • Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 

                                                        9. Babusar Pass

                                                        • Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit

                                                        10. Kohat Pass

                                                        • Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 

                                                        11. Khojak Pass

                                                        • Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman

                                                        12. Tochi Pass

                                                        • Connects: Ghazni with Bannu

                                                        Last verified on 01-04-2026

                                                        397

                                                        Siachen Glacier is situated in which mountain range?

                                                        Correct Answer: Karakorum
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation
                                                        • Siachen Glacier length is 76KM
                                                        • Located in the eastern Karakoram range of the Himalayas,
                                                        • Located in Ghanche District, Gilgit-Baltistan.
                                                        • Height: 20,000 feet
                                                        •  Pakistan's largest Glacier
                                                        • World 2nd largest Glacier

                                                        400

                                                        Who was appointed by Quaid-e-Azam as a first ambassador of Pakistan to Iran?

                                                        Correct Answer: Raja Ghazsnfer Ali
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation
                                                        • Iran was the first country to recognize Pakistan as a sovereign nation after its independence in August 1947.
                                                        • Quaid-e-Azam appointed Raja Ghazanfar Ali Khan as Pakistan's first ambassador to Iran.
                                                        • Prime Minister Liaquat Ali Khan made his first state visit to Iran in May 1949.
                                                        • The Shah of Iran became the first foreign head of state to visit Pakistan in March 1950.

                                                        401

                                                        When Faiz Ahmad Faiz Awarded Lenin Peace Prize?

                                                        Correct Answer: 1962
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation
                                                        • Faiz Ahmed Faiz was the first Asian poet to receive the Lenin Peace Prize.
                                                        • He was awarded in 1962 by the Soviet Union for his contributions to literature and peace.
                                                        • He was also nominated for the Nobel Prize in Literature.
                                                        • He wrote extensively in Urdu and Punjabi.
                                                        • Born: February 13, 1911, Narowal, Pakistan
                                                        • Died: November 20, 1984, Lahore, Pakistan

                                                        Correct Answer: 1980
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation

                                                        • Established: 1980
                                                        • Total Judges: 8
                                                        • Ulema Judges: 3 (mandatory)
                                                        • Tenure of Judges: 3 years (extendable by the President)
                                                        • Founder: General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq

                                                        Who founded Muhammadan Literary Society in 1863?

                                                        Correct Answer: Nawab Abdul Latif
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation

                                                        • It aimed to promote modern education and literary development among Muslims in India.
                                                        • The society was located at Nawab Abdul Latif’s residence, 16 Taltala, Kolkata.

                                                        404

                                                        In Tashkent Deceleration who was foreign minister of Pakistan?

                                                        Correct Answer: Z.A. Bhutto
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation
                                                        • The Tashkent Declaration was signed on 10 January 1966.
                                                        • It ended the 1965 Indo-Pak war through peace talks.
                                                        • At that time, Zulfikar Ali Bhutto was the Foreign Minister of Pakistan.

                                                        The agreement was mediated by the Soviet Union.

                                                        • Pakistan leader: Ayub Khan
                                                        • India leader: Lal Bahadur Shastri

                                                        405

                                                        What was the real name of Tipu Sultan or Tiger of Mysore?

                                                        Correct Answer: Fateh Ali
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation
                                                        • Tipu Sultan, also known as the Tiger of Mysore.
                                                        • Fateh Ali was the real name of Tipu Sultan or Tiger of Mysore.
                                                        • He was the ruler of the Kingdom of Mysore based in South India.
                                                        • He was Born: on December 1, 1751, in Devanahalli, India.
                                                        • He was Died: on May 4, 1799, in Srirangapatna, India.
                                                        • He wrote a letter to France to help him against British Army rule in India. 
                                                        • The third battle of Mysore is also known as Tipu Sultan`s battle against the British.

                                                        Last verified on 01-04-2026

                                                        406

                                                        Mohammed Bin Qasim conquered Sindh in the year _____?

                                                        Correct Answer: 712 AD
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation

                                                        Muhammad ibn Qasim

                                                        • Conquest of Sindh: 712 AD
                                                        • Battle: Aror (near Rohri)
                                                        • Opponent: Raja Dahir (killed in battle)
                                                        • He attacked Sindh on orders of the Umayyad Caliph through Hajjaj bin Yusuf.
                                                        • Death: 18 July 715 AD

                                                        Major cities captured:

                                                        • Nirun
                                                        • Debal (Sikka fortress)
                                                        • Brahmanabad
                                                        • Alor (Aror)
                                                        • Multan

                                                        Last verified on 01-04-2026

                                                        407

                                                        A woman who fought in 1857 War of Independence?

                                                        Correct Answer: Rani of Jhansi
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation
                                                        • The woman who fought in the 1857 War of Independence is Rani of Jhansi. 
                                                        •  Rani Lakshmibai, also known as the Rani of Jhansi,
                                                        • She was a prominent figure in the Indian Rebellion of 1857.
                                                        • She led the resistance against British rule in Jhansi, Uttar Pradesh.
                                                        Last verified on 01-04-2026
                                                        408

                                                        The second highest peak K-2 in the world is located in?

                                                        Correct Answer: Karakoram Range
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation
                                                        • Mount K2 is the second-highest mountain on Earth.
                                                        • It is part of the Karakorum Range.
                                                        • It is located on the China–Pakistan border between Baltistan in the Gilgit-Baltistan region of northern Pakistan.
                                                        • K2, at 8,611 meters above sea level.
                                                        • K-2 is Also Known As Godwin Austin.
                                                        • 'K' standing for Karakoram.
                                                        • K2 mountain was first measured by Godwin Austen in 1856.
                                                        • The Silk Route is also known as the Karakoram Highway.

                                                        Additional information:

                                                        • The first highest mountain on Earth is Mount Everest.
                                                        • It is located in Nepal.
                                                        • The new height of Mount Everest is 8,848.86m (29,032 feet).
                                                        • Mount Everest was named after George Everest in 1856.

                                                        Last verified on 01-04-2026

                                                        409

                                                        Name the Mughal King who died in 1707?

                                                        Correct Answer: Aurangzeb
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation
                                                        • Aurangzeb Died on March 3, 1707, Bhingar, Ahmednagar, India.
                                                        • He is buried in a humble tomb in grave in Khulabad.
                                                        • He was the sixth Mughal emperor.
                                                        • He ruled the Mughal Empire from 1658 to 1707.
                                                        • He Born on November 3, 1618, Dahod, India.

                                                        410

                                                        Who is the first Punjabi poet?

                                                        Correct Answer: Baba Fareed
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation
                                                        • Baba Farid is considered the first Punjabi poet.
                                                        • He was born in 1173 and died on 7 May 1266.
                                                        • His birthplace is Kothewal village near Multan.
                                                        • He spread Sufi teachings through poetry and songs.
                                                        • His shrine is located in Pakpattan, Pakistan.

                                                        Additional information:

                                                        • Hazrat Hassan ibn Sabit (R.A): First poet of Islam
                                                        • Allama Iqbal: National Poet of Pakistan, called “Poet of the East”
                                                        • Amir Kror Suri: First poet of Pashto language

                                                        • Renamed King Edward Medical College in 1911
                                                        • Got university status in 2005

                                                        412

                                                        Write the name of the Muslim ruler who first laid the foundation of the Islamic state in the subcontinent?

                                                        Correct Answer: Qutub-uddin-Aibak
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation
                                                      • Qutub-ud-Din Aibak (1206) was the first Muslim ruler to establish an Islamic state in the Indian subcontinent, founding the Delhi Sultanate.
                                                      • He was a general of Muhammad Ghori.
                                                      • He became the first Sultan of Delhi after Ghori’s death.
                                                      • Last verified on 01-04-2026
                                                        • 413

                                                        Pakistan purchased Gwadar port from which country?

                                                        Correct Answer: Oman
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation
                                                        • The Gwadar Port is situated on the Arabian Sea at Gwadar in the Balochistan, Pakistan.
                                                        • Omani Rule: Gwadar remained under Oman for 174 years (1783–1958).
                                                        • Purchase Date: 8 September 1958 by Pakistan.
                                                        • Official Integration: 8 December 1958.
                                                        • Port Opening: 2002 (development of the modern deep-sea port began).

                                                        Additional information:

                                                        Pakistan has three big sea ports:

                                                        1. Karachi Port
                                                        2. Muhammad Bin Qasim port
                                                        3. Gwadar port

                                                        Last verified on 16-03-2026

                                                        414

                                                        The Picture on the back of the 5000 Rupee Pakistani Note?

                                                        Correct Answer: Faisal Mosque
                                                        Show explanation
                                                        Explanation
                                                        • Pakistani currency notes feature important national landmarks on the back side.
                                                          • The State Bank of Pakistan issued first banknotes on 1 October 1948.
                                                          • Printed by: Thomas De La Rue & Company (UK)

                                                          Additional information:

                                                          • Rs. 10: Khyber Pass
                                                          • Rs. 100: Quaid-e-Azam Residency
                                                          • Rs. 500: Badshahi Mosque
                                                          • Rs. 1000: Islamia College Peshawar
                                                          • Rs. 5000: Faisal Mosque

                                                          Last verified on 01-04-2026

                                                          Correct Answer: Iran

                                                          416

                                                          Punjab Culture Day is celebrated on which date?

                                                          Correct Answer: March 14
                                                          Show explanation
                                                          Explanation

                                                          Punjab Culture Day is celebrated on 14 March.

                                                          Balochi Culture Day is celebrated on March 2.

                                                          Sindhi Culture Day is celebrated on first Sunday of December.

                                                          Pashtun Culture Day is celebrated on September 23.

                                                          417

                                                          Which of the following is Higher Rank in Pakistan Air Force?

                                                          Correct Answer: Air commodore
                                                          Show explanation
                                                          Explanation
                                                          • Pakistan Air Force has a structured rank hierarchy.
                                                            • Air Commodore is the highest rank in this list.
                                                            • It is a one-star air officer rank, above Wing Commander.

                                                            Additional information:

                                                            • The Pakistan Air Force was established on 14 August 1947.
                                                            • It is the part of Pakistan Armed Forces.
                                                            • It is headquarters Air Headquarters (AHQ), Islamabad.

                                                            Last verified on 31-03-2026

                                                            418

                                                            Trango Tower is a _____?

                                                            Correct Answer: Mountain peak of Pakistan
                                                            Show explanation
                                                            Explanation
                                                            • Trango Tower is a mountain peak located in the Baltistan region of Pakistan.
                                                            • It stands at a height of 6,339 meters (20,807 feet).
                                                            • It is known for its steep and challenging climb.

                                                            Last verified on 31-03-2026

                                                            419

                                                            The narrow strip in Afghanistan that separates Pakistan from Tajikistan is called _____?

                                                            Correct Answer: Wakhan Corridor
                                                            Show explanation
                                                            Explanation
                                                            • The narrow strip is called the Wakhan Corridor.
                                                            • It is located in northeastern Afghanistan.
                                                            • It separates Pakistan from Tajikistan.
                                                            • This corridor was created as a buffer zone during the 19th century (Great Game).
                                                            • Length: ~350 km
                                                            • Width: 13–65 km (approx.)

                                                            Last verified on 31-03-2026

                                                            420

                                                            Manchhar is a ____?

                                                            Correct Answer: Lake
                                                            Show explanation
                                                            Explanation
                                                            • Manchhar Lake is the largest natural freshwater lake in Jamshoro Sindh Pakistan.
                                                            • It collects water from the Kithar mountain range.
                                                            • It is one of South Asia's largest.
                                                            • It covers an area of 23.5 km (14.6 mi)
                                                            • It is the part of Indus River basin.

                                                            Last verified on 31-03-2026

                                                            421

                                                            From Karachi farthest point on the coastal highway is _______?

                                                            Correct Answer: Jiwani
                                                            Show explanation
                                                            Explanation
                                                            • Jiwani is the farthest coastal point from Karachi on the highway.
                                                            • Although the highway officially ends at Gwadar, Jiwani lies further west near the Iran border.

                                                            Additional information:

                                                            • The Makran Coastal Highway connects Karachi to Gwadar.
                                                            • It ends near the Iran border in Balochistan.
                                                            • It passes the towns of Ormara and Pasni, both in Balochistan.
                                                            • The highway provides a scenic coastal route across the Arabian Sea.
                                                            • Length: 653 km (406 miles)
                                                            • Start: Karachi (Sindh)
                                                            • End: Jiwani (Balochistan)
                                                            • Construction: 2002–2004

                                                            Last verified on 31-03-2026

                                                            422

                                                            Lowari pass connects Dir with ______?

                                                            Correct Answer: Chitral
                                                            Show explanation
                                                            Explanation
                                                            • The Lowari Pass connects the regions of Chitral and Dir within Pakistan's Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa province,
                                                            • It is a high mountain pass located in KPK
                                                            •  Its closure due to snow (late November to late May) 

                                                              Additional information RELATED PASSES:

                                                              • Babusar Pass – Connects Kaghan with Chilas
                                                              • Shandur Pass – Connects Chitral with Gilgit
                                                              • Malakand Pass – Connects Mardan with Swat
                                                              • Khunjerab Pass – Connects Pakistan with China

                                                              Last Updated on 14-01-2026

                                                              423

                                                              Waris Shah the author of "Heer Ranjha" is buried in _____?

                                                              Correct Answer: Jandiala Sher Khan
                                                              Show explanation
                                                              Explanation
                                                              • The tomb or mausoleum of Waris Shah is near Sheikhupura, Punjab, Pakistan in Jandiala Sher Khan town.
                                                              • He was born in January 23, 1722.
                                                              • He was passed away in 1798.

                                                              Last verified on 31-03-2026

                                                              424

                                                              Pakistan successfully tested five nuclear devices at Chaghi on _____?

                                                              Correct Answer: May 28 1998
                                                              Show explanation
                                                              Explanation
                                                              • Pakistan conducted a series of nuclear tests on 28 May 1998 at the Chagai test site, in Balochistan.
                                                              • 5 Nuclear tests were conducted by Pakistan at 15:15 hrs PST on 28 May 1998.
                                                              • The tests were performed at Ras Koh Hills in the Chagai District of Balochistan Province.
                                                              • Chagai-I was Pakistan's first public test of nuclear weapons.

                                                                Additional information:

                                                                • Chagai-II is the codename assigned to the second atomic test conducted by Pakistan, carried out on 30 May 1998.
                                                                • These tests followed the Chagai-I series.
                                                                • The test site was Kharan Desert, Pakistan.
                                                                • The number of tests is 1.

                                                                Last verified on 30-03-2026

                                                                425

                                                                Name the Mughal Emperor whose Revenue Minister was Todar Mal?

                                                                Correct Answer: Akbar
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation
                                                                • Todar Mal was the Revenue Minister of Akbar, the third Mughal Emperor, who ruled from 1556 to 1605.
                                                                • Todar Mal is credited with reforming the revenue administration and introducing the "Zabti" system.
                                                                • It helped to increase state revenue and promote agricultural development.

                                                                Last verified on 31-03-2026

                                                                426

                                                                The Battle of Plassey was fought in 1757 between?

                                                                Correct Answer: Lord Clive and Nawab Siraj-ud-Daula
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation
                                                                • The Battle of Plassey was fought on 23 June 1757.
                                                                • It took place at Plassey (Palashi) near the Hooghly River.
                                                                • The battle was between the British East India Company and Siraj-ud-Daulah.
                                                                • The British were led by Robert Clive.
                                                                • It marked the beginning of British control in India (Bengal).

                                                                Last verified on 31-03-2026

                                                                427

                                                                What is the longest glacier in the Karakoram and second-longest in the world’s non-polar areas?

                                                                Correct Answer: Siachen Glacier
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation
                                                                • The Siachen Glacier is the longest glacier in the Karakoram Range.
                                                                • It is the second-longest glacier in the world outside polar regions.
                                                                • It is located in the eastern Karakoram (Himalayan region).
                                                                • Its length is about 76 km.

                                                                Last verified on 31-03-2026

                                                                428

                                                                When Shalimar garden was established?

                                                                Correct Answer: 1642
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation

                                                                • Shalimar Gardens in Lahore was built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan.
                                                                • Construction Year: 1641–42
                                                                • Included in UNESCO World Heritage Site: 1981
                                                                • Terraces: 3 main terraces (upper, middle, lower)
                                                                • Water Fountains: Over 400 fountains
                                                                • Total Area: Approximately 16 hectares
                                                                • Shah Jahan also built: Taj Mahal (Agra), Jama Masjid (Delhi), Red Fort (Delhi)

                                                                Last verified on 18-01-2026


                                                                Sr#

                                                                Built by

                                                                Name of Places

                                                                1

                                                                Jahangir​

                                                                Shalimar Bagh, Kashmir

                                                                2

                                                                Shah Jahan

                                                                1. Shalimar Garden, Pakistan,

                                                                 

                                                                2. Taj Mahal,

                                                                3. the Jama Masjid,

                                                                4. the Red Fort

                                                                3

                                                                Akbar

                                                                1.    Buland Darwaza,

                                                                2.    Fatehpur Sikri

                                                                4

                                                                Aurangzeb

                                                                Moti Masjid, Delhi

                                                                429

                                                                Which foreign head of state was the first to visit Pakistan after independence?

                                                                Correct Answer: Shah of Iran
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation
                                                                • The first foreign head of state to visit Pakistan was the Mohammad Reza Pahlavi.
                                                                • He visited Pakistan in March 1950.
                                                                • This visit strengthened early diplomatic relations between Pakistan and Iran.
                                                                • Iran was also among the first countries to recognize Pakistan in 1947.

                                                                Last verified on 31-03-2026

                                                                430

                                                                Thal desert is located in _____?

                                                                Correct Answer: Punjab
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation

                                                                Thal Desert

                                                                • Location: East Punjab 
                                                                • Between: Indus & Jhelum rivers 
                                                                • Length: 190 miles

                                                                Last verified on 30-03-2026

                                                                431

                                                                Mahmud of Ghazni attacked India how many times?

                                                                Correct Answer: 17 times
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation
                                                                • Mahmud of Ghazni attacked India 17 times.
                                                                • Most famous raid was in 1025 AD, when he plundered the Somnath temple in Gujarat.
                                                                • Last (17th) attack took place in 1027 AD.
                                                                • Known as “Abductor of Scholars” due to looting knowledge centers.
                                                                • Played a significant role in Turkish invasions of India.

                                                                Last verified on 30-03-2026

                                                                432

                                                                The Indus Water Treaty was signed during the regime of _______?

                                                                Correct Answer: Ayub Khan
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation
                                                                • The Indus Waters Treaty was signed on September 19, 1960, in Karachi.
                                                                • It was signed between Pakistan (President Ayub Khan) and India (Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru).
                                                                • It was brokered by the World Bank.

                                                                It became effective on April 1, 1960.

                                                                • Western Rivers (given to Pakistan): Indus, Jhelum, Chenab
                                                                • Eastern Rivers (given to India): Ravi, Beas, Sutlej

                                                                Last verified on 30-03-2026

                                                                433

                                                                Gandhara civilization is in _____?

                                                                Correct Answer: Pakistan
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation
                                                                • The Gandhara civilization was located in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.
                                                                • It is particularly in the region around Taxila and Peshawar.
                                                                • It was a major center of Buddhist culture.
                                                                • It is art from around the 6th century BCE to the 11th century CE.

                                                                Last verified on 30-03-2026

                                                                434

                                                                The partition of Bengal was made in 1905, it was annulled in?

                                                                Correct Answer: 1911
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation
                                                                • The Partition of Bengal, implemented in 1905 by Lord Curzon.
                                                                • It was formally annulled in 1911. 
                                                                • He divided Bengal into two provinces on 16-10-1905.

                                                                  The province was divided into:

                                                                  • West Bengal, which had a majority of Hindus,
                                                                  • East Bengal, which had a majority of Muslims

                                                                  Last verified on 30-03-2026

                                                                  435

                                                                  Tarbela Dam is build on which river?

                                                                  Correct Answer: Indus
                                                                  Show explanation
                                                                  Explanation
                                                                  • Tarbela Dam was built on the Indus River in KPk.
                                                                  • It is located mainly in the Swabi District Tehsil Topi of KPK.
                                                                  • It is the largest earth-filled dam in the world
                                                                  • The Construction began in 1968  
                                                                  • They were Opened in 1976
                                                                  • Height: 470 feet (143 m)

                                                                  Last verified on 30-03-2026

                                                                  436

                                                                  Rai Ahmed Khan Kharal was born in 1803 in Jhamra, a village of ________ district.

                                                                  Correct Answer: Okara
                                                                  Show explanation
                                                                  Explanation

                                                                  • Birth Place: Jhamra (Okara District)
                                                                  • Nearby Area: Gogera Bangla
                                                                  • He was a freedom fighter of the 1857 War of Independence.
